MONDO:0005147.title: type 1 diabetes mellitus
MONDO:0005147.description: A lifelong condition where the pancreas produces little or no insulin, requiring daily insulin injections.

MONDO:0005148.title: type 2 diabetes mellitus
MONDO:0005148.description: The most common form of diabetes where the body doesn't use insulin properly, leading to high blood sugar. Often develops gradually and is linked to lifestyle and genetics.

MONDO:0004975.title: Alzheimer disease
MONDO:0004975.description: A progressive brain disease that causes memory loss, confusion, and changes in thinking and behavior. The most common cause of dementia.

excluded_pgs.method: log-OR weighted sum of risk allele dosages

MONDO:0005180.title: Parkinson disease
MONDO:0005180.description: A brain disorder that causes tremors, stiffness, and difficulty with movement and balance. Symptoms worsen over time.

MONDO:0001657.title: brain cancer
MONDO:0001657.description: Cancer that starts in the brain or spreads to the brain from other parts of the body.

MONDO:0005575.title: colorectal cancer
MONDO:0005575.description: Cancer that develops in the colon (large intestine) or rectum, often starting as small growths called polyps.

MONDO:0001627.title: dementia
MONDO:0001627.description: A decline in memory, thinking, and reasoning skills that interferes with daily activities. Can be caused by various brain conditions.

excluded_pgs.method: integrative PRS of 24 component PRSs derived from LDPRED-2, C+T, PRS-CS

MONDO:0004979.title: asthma
MONDO:0004979.description: A chronic condition where airways become inflamed and narrow, causing wheezing, coughing, chest tightness, and difficulty breathing.

EFO_0007825.title: balding measurement
EFO_0007825.description: A measure of hair loss or balding patterns, often related to genetics and hormones.

EFO_0007585.title: Cannabis use
EFO_0007585.description: The use of cannabis (marijuana) for recreational or medicinal purposes.

MONDO:0005277.title: migraine disorder
MONDO:0005277.description: Severe recurring headaches often accompanied by nausea, vomiting, and sensitivity to light and sound.

MONDO:0005041.title: glaucoma
MONDO:0005041.description: A group of eye diseases that damage the optic nerve, often due to increased pressure in the eye, potentially causing blindness.

MONDO:0004985.title: bipolar disorder
MONDO:0004985.description: A mental health condition causing extreme mood swings between periods of depression and elevated mood (mania).

MONDO:0002050.title: depressive disorder
MONDO:0002050.description: A mental health condition causing persistent feelings of sadness, hopelessness, and loss of interest in activities.

MONDO:0007079.title: alcohol dependence
MONDO:0007079.description: A chronic disease where a person cannot control their drinking despite negative consequences to their health and life.

MONDO:0002326.title: alcohol-induced mental disorder
MONDO:0002326.description: Mental health problems caused by excessive alcohol use, including memory problems and changes in thinking.

MONDO:0005090.title: schizophrenia
MONDO:0005090.description: A serious mental health condition causing hallucinations, delusions, disorganized thinking, and difficulty distinguishing reality.

EFO_0004612.title: high density lipoprotein cholesterol measurement
EFO_0004612.description: A measurement of 'good' cholesterol in the blood, which helps remove other forms of cholesterol and reduce heart disease risk.

EFO_0004530.title: triglyceride measurement
EFO_0004530.description: A measurement of fats in the blood that can increase heart disease risk when levels are too high.

EFO_0004574.title: total cholesterol measurement
EFO_0004574.description: A measurement of all cholesterol types in the blood, used to assess cardiovascular disease risk.

OBA_1000968.title: vitamin D level
OBA_1000968.description: The amount of vitamin D in the body, important for bone health and immune function.

EFO_0003913.title: angina pectoris
EFO_0003913.description: Chest pain or discomfort caused by reduced blood flow to the heart muscle, often triggered by physical activity or stress.

MONDO:0004986.title: urinary bladder carcinoma
MONDO:0004986.description: Cancer that develops in the bladder, the organ that stores urine.

MONDO:0005271.title: allergic disease
MONDO:0005271.description: Conditions where the immune system overreacts to normally harmless substances, causing symptoms like sneezing, rashes, or breathing problems.

EFO_0006925.title: lipoprotein A measurement
EFO_0006925.description: A measurement of a specific type of cholesterol particle that can increase heart disease risk when levels are high.

EFO_0004697.title: estradiol measurement
EFO_0004697.description: A measurement of estradiol, a female hormone important for reproductive health and bone strength.

MONDO:0007915.title: systemic lupus erythematosus
MONDO:0007915.description: An autoimmune disease that can affect multiple organs, causing joint pain, skin rashes, and organ damage.

EFO_0004908.title: testosterone measurement
EFO_0004908.description: A measurement of testosterone, a hormone important for male sexual development and function.

EFO_0005140.title: autoimmune disease
EFO_0005140.description: Conditions where the immune system mistakenly attacks the body's own healthy tissues and organs.

HP_0002140.title: Ischemic stroke
HP_0002140.description: A type of stroke caused by blocked blood flow to the brain, often due to blood clots.

excluded_pgs.method: Integrative PGS (also referred to as metaGRS) of 22 component PGSs
Integrative PGS (also referred to as metaGRS) of 22 component PGSs

MONDO:0005301.title: multiple sclerosis
MONDO:0005301.description: An autoimmune disease that damages the protective covering of nerve fibers, causing communication problems between the brain and body.

HP_0002619.title: Varicose veins
HP_0002619.description: Enlarged, twisted veins that are visible under the skin, most commonly in the legs.

EFO_0009959.title: diverticular disease
EFO_0009959.description: A condition where small pouches form in the colon wall, which can become inflamed or infected.

EFO_0010733.title: polyunsaturated fatty acid measurement
EFO_0010733.description: A measurement of healthy fats in the blood that are important for heart and brain health.

EFO_0004615.title: apolipoprotein B measurement
EFO_0004615.description: A measurement of a protein that carries cholesterol in the blood, used to assess heart disease risk.

MONDO:0018906.title: follicular lymphoma
MONDO:0018906.description: A slow-growing type of blood cancer that affects B-cells, a type of white blood cell.

MONDO:0001134.title: essential hypertension
MONDO:0001134.description: High blood pressure with no identifiable cause, the most common type of hypertension.

MONDO:0024431.title: bilirubin metabolism disease
MONDO:0024431.description: A condition where the body has trouble processing bilirubin, leading to yellowing of the skin and eyes.

MONDO:0021398.title: polyp of rectum
MONDO:0021398.description: Non-cancerous growths that develop in the rectum.

MONDO:0008315.title: prostate cancer
MONDO:0008315.description: Cancer that develops in the prostate gland in men.

HP_0001279.title: Syncope
HP_0001279.description: Temporary loss of consciousness due to reduced blood flow to the brain, often called fainting.

MONDO:0003916.title: overnutrition
MONDO:0003916.description: Consuming too many calories or nutrients, often leading to obesity and related health problems.

EFO_1001516.title: ovarian serous carcinoma
EFO_1001516.description: A specific type of ovarian cancer that develops from the cells lining the ovary.

MONDO:0000632.title: uterine benign neoplasm
MONDO:0000632.description: Non-cancerous tumors that develop in the uterus.

EFO_1000243.title: Epidermal Inclusion Cyst
EFO_1000243.description: Small, non-cancerous cysts that develop under the skin, containing keratin and other materials.

MONDO:0021661.title: coronary atherosclerosis
MONDO:0021661.description: Hardening and narrowing of the coronary arteries due to plaque buildup.

MONDO:0001056.title: gastric cancer
MONDO:0001056.description: Cancer that develops in the stomach.

MONDO:0015263.title: Brugada syndrome
MONDO:0015263.description: A genetic heart condition that can cause dangerous irregular heartbeats.

MONDO:0000385.title: benign digestive system neoplasm
MONDO:0000385.description: Non-cancerous tumors that develop in the digestive system.

EFO_0000768.title: idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis
EFO_0000768.description: A lung disease where scar tissue builds up, making it hard to breathe.

MONDO:0002278.title: benign colon neoplasm
MONDO:0002278.description: Non-cancerous growths that develop in the colon.

MONDO:0002279.title: iron metabolism disease
MONDO:0002279.description: Conditions affecting how the body processes and uses iron.

MONDO:0001409.title: esophagitis
MONDO:0001409.description: Inflammation of the esophagus, often caused by acid reflux.

MONDO:0060766.title: anal polyp
MONDO:0060766.description: Small growths that develop in the anal area.

MONDO:0004670.title: lupus erythematosus
MONDO:0004670.description: Autoimmune conditions that cause inflammation of connective tissues.

MONDO:0004596.title: cor pulmonale
MONDO:0004596.description: Heart problems caused by lung disease that increases pressure in the pulmonary arteries.

EFO_0010820.title: spontaneous coronary artery dissection
EFO_0010820.description: A rare condition where the wall of a coronary artery tears spontaneously.

EFO_0004723.title: coronary artery calcification
EFO_0004723.description: Calcium deposits in the coronary arteries, a sign of atherosclerosis.

EFO_0009460.title: ACPA-negative rheumatoid arthritis
EFO_0009460.description: A type of rheumatoid arthritis that tests negative for certain antibodies.

EFO_0009459.title: ACPA-positive rheumatoid arthritis
EFO_0009459.description: A type of rheumatoid arthritis that tests positive for certain antibodies.

EFO_0007848.title: apolipoprotein A-IV measurement
EFO_0007848.description: A measurement of apolipoprotein A-IV, a protein involved in fat metabolism and transport.

MONDO:0001187.title: urinary bladder cancer
MONDO:0001187.description: Cancer that develops in the bladder, the organ that stores urine.

MONDO:0004627.title: duodenitis
MONDO:0004627.description: Inflammation of the duodenum, the first part of the small intestine, often causing stomach pain.

EFO_0000195.title: metabolic syndrome
EFO_0000195.description: A group of conditions including high blood pressure, high blood sugar, excess belly fat, and abnormal cholesterol levels that increase disease risk.

EFO_1001958.title: high grade ovarian serous adenocarcinoma
EFO_1001958.description: An aggressive type of ovarian cancer that develops from the cells lining the ovary.

EFO_0010176.title: keratinocyte carcinoma
EFO_0010176.description: A skin cancer arising from the keratin-producing cells of the epidermis. The term encompasses both basal cell cancer and squamous cell cancer.

MONDO:0021116.title: luminal A breast carcinoma
MONDO:0021116.description: A biologic subset of breast cancer defined by high expression of genes characteristic of luminal epithelial cells, including estrogen receptor (ER), estrogen regulated protein LIV-1, and the transcription factors hepatocyte nuclear factor 3, HNF3A, XBP1, and GATA 3. This subtype of breast cancer is associated with a good prognosis.

MONDO:0021115.title: luminal B breast carcinoma
MONDO:0021115.description: A biologic subset of breast cancer defined by low to moderate expression of genes characteristic of luminal epithelial cells including estrogen receptor (ER), and high expression of GGH, LAPTM4B, and CCNE1. This subtype of breast cancer is associated with a good prognosis, although not as favorable as the luminal A subtype.

HP_0001634.title: Mitral valve prolapse
HP_0001634.description: One or both of the leaflets (cusps) of the mitral valve bulges back into the left atrium upon contraction of the left ventricle.

MONDO:0005184.title: pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma
MONDO:0005184.description: An infiltrating glandular cancer that arises from the epithelial cells of the pancreas. It affects males more often than females and the patients are usually over 50 years of age. Microscopically it is characterized by the presence of glandular (ductal) differentiation and desmoplastic stroma formation. Signs and symptoms include pain, loss of weight, and jaundice. It grows rapidly and is usually detected after it has metastasized to other anatomic sites. The prognosis is usually poor.

MONDO:0021400.title: polyp of colon
MONDO:0021400.description: A polyp that involves the colon.

MONDO:0018177.title: glioblastoma
MONDO:0018177.description: A malignant astrocytoma characterized by the presence of small areas of necrotizing tissue that is surrounded by anaplastic cells as well as the presence of hyperplastic blood vessels, and that has a poor prognosis.

MONDO:0005130.title: celiac disease
MONDO:0005130.description: An autoimmune genetic disorder with an unknown pattern of inheritance that primarily affects the digestive tract. It is caused by intolerance to dietary gluten. Consumption of gluten protein triggers an immune response which damages small intestinal villi and prevents adequate absorption of nutrients. Clinical signs include abdominal cramping, diarrhea or constipation and weight loss. If untreated, the clinical course may progress to malnutrition, anemia, osteoporosis and an increased risk of intestinal malignancies. However, the prognosis is favorable with successful avoidance of gluten in the diet. [NCIT: C26714]

MONDO:0005350.title: Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
MONDO:0005350.description: Enlargement and ballooning of the vessel that supplies arterial blood to the abdomen, pelvis and legs. [NCIT: P378]

HP_0002027.title: Abdominal pain
HP_0002027.description: An unpleasant sensation characterized by physical discomfort (such as pricking, throbbing, or aching) and perceived to originate in the abdomen.

HP_0003119.title: Abnormal circulating lipid concentration
HP_0003119.description: Any deviation from the normal concentration of a lipid in the blood circulation.

HP_0001000.title: Abnormality of skin pigmentation
HP_0001000.description: An abnormality of the pigmentation of the skin.

HP_0000924.title: Abnormality of the skeletal system
HP_0000924.description: An abnormality of the skeletal system.

MONDO:0002492.title: acute kidney injury
MONDO:0002492.description: Sudden and sustained deterioration of the kidney function characterized by decreased glomerular filtration rate, increased serum creatinine or oliguria. [NCIT: C26808]

MONDO:0004967.title: acute lymphoblastic leukemia
MONDO:0004967.description: Leukemia with an acute onset, characterized by the presence of lymphoblasts in the bone marrow and the peripheral blood. It includes the acute B lymphoblastic leukemia and acute T lymphoblastic leukemia. [NCIT: C3167]

MONDO:0004781.title: acute myocardial infarction
MONDO:0004781.description: Necrosis of the myocardium, as a result of interruption of the blood supply to the area. It is characterized by a severe and rapid onset of symptoms that may include chest pain, often radiating to the left arm and left side of the neck, dyspnea, sweating, and palpitations. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_0004347.title: addictive behaviour
EFO_0004347.description: The observable, measurable, and often pathological activity of an organism that portrays its inability to overcome a habit resulting in an insatiable craving for a substance or for performing certain acts. The addictive behavior includes the emotional and physical overdependence on the object of habit in increasing amount or frequency.

OBA_VT0005452.title: adipose amount
OBA_VT0005452.description: The amount of a adipose tissue. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

EFO_0004918.title: age at diagnosis
EFO_0004918.description: The age, measured from some defined time point e.g. birth at which a subject (e.g. a human patient) is diagnosed with some disease e.g. breast cancer.

EFO_0009101.title: age at first birth measurement
EFO_0009101.description: A measurement of the age at which a woman gave birth to her first live child

EFO_0009749.title: age at first sexual intercourse measurement
EFO_0009749.description: A measurement of the age at which a person has the first sexual intercourse.

EFO_0021784.title: age at initiation of smoking
EFO_0021784.description: Age of initation of the habit of smoking, the inhaling and exhaling of tobacco smoke.

EFO_0004703.title: age at menarche
EFO_0004703.description: The age of onset of (human) menarche, the beginning of the first menstrual cycle in an individual

EFO_0004704.title: age at menopause
EFO_0004704.description: The age of onset of (human) menopause, the beginning of the first menstrual cycle in an individual

MONDO:0043765.title: age-related hearing impairment
MONDO:0043765.description: Bilateral hearing loss caused by progressive degeneration of cochlear structures and central auditory pathways, typically associated with the aging process. [NCIT: C116367]

MONDO:0005150.title: age-related macular degeneration
MONDO:0005150.description: Age-related loss of vision in the central portion of the retina (macula), secondary to retinal degeneration. [NCIT: P378]

OBA_0005494.title: aging rate
OBA_0005494.description: The rate of the aging process.

OBA_1000840.title: alcohol consumption quality
OBA_1000840.description: The quality of a alcohol consumption. [obo: oba/patterns/entity_attribute_part_of]

EFO_0007835.title: alcohol dependence measurement
EFO_0007835.description: quantification of some aspect of alcohol dependence or misuse, eg using a cummulative symptom score to determine the severity of a case of alcohol dependence

EFO_0004329.title: alcohol drinking
EFO_0004329.description: Behaviors associated with the ingesting of alcoholic beverages, including social drinking.

MONDO:0006644.title: alcoholic liver cirrhosis
MONDO:0006644.description: A disorder of the liver characterized by the presence of fibrotic scar tissue instead of healthy liver tissue. This condition is attributed to excessive consumption of alcoholic beverages. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_0009458.title: alcohol use disorder measurement
EFO_0009458.description: A quantitative measure of alcohol use disorder, e.g. using the Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test.

EFO_0004533.title: alkaline phosphatase measurement
EFO_0004533.description: Alkaline phosphatase measurement is a quantification of alkaline phosphatase in blood. AP is a marker for bone and liver disease.

MONDO:0011786.title: allergic rhinitis
MONDO:0011786.description: Inflammation of the nasal mucous membranes caused by an IgE-mediated response to external allergens. The inflammation may also involve the mucous membranes of the sinuses, eyes, middle ear, and pharynx. Symptoms include sneezing, nasal congestion, rhinorrhea, and itching. It may lead to fatigue, drowsiness, and malaise thus causing impairment of the quality of life. [NCIT: C79532]

MONDO:0002280.title: anemia
MONDO:0002280.description: A reduction in the number of red blood cells, the amount of hemoglobin, and/or the volume of packed red blood cells. Clinically, anemia represents a reduction in the oxygen-transporting capacity of a designated volume of blood, resulting from an imbalance between blood loss (through hemorrhage or hemolysis) and blood production. Signs and symptoms of anemia may include pallor of the skin and mucous membranes, shortness of breath, palpitations of the heart, soft systolic murmurs, lethargy, and fatigability. [NCIT: C2869]

MONDO:0005306.title: ankylosing spondylitis
MONDO:0005306.description: An autoimmune chronic inflammatory disorder characterized by inflammation in the vertebral joints of the spine and sacroiliac joints. It predominantly affects young males. Patients present with stiffness and pain in the spine. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_0004302.title: anthropometric measurement
EFO_0004302.description: Anthropometry is a measurement of the size, weight, and proportions of the human or other primate body.

MONDO:0005618.title: anxiety disorder
MONDO:0005618.description: A category of psychiatric disorders which are characterized by anxious feelings or fear often accompanied by physical symptoms associated with anxiety. [NCIT: C2878]

EFO_0009863.title: anxiety measurement
EFO_0009863.description: Quantification of the level of a generalised feeling of anxiety experienced by an individual, typically measured via a questionnaire.

EFO_0020865.title: aortic measurement
EFO_0020865.description: Measurement of some parts of the aorta, including ascending aorta distensibility and area.

MONDO:0003803.title: aortic valve disease
MONDO:0003803.description: A disease involving the aortic valve.

EFO_0007659.title: APOE carrier status
EFO_0007659.description: quantification of the presence or absence of apolipoprotein E (APOE) in an individual

EFO_0004614.title: apolipoprotein A 1 measurement
EFO_0004614.description: Is a quantification of apolipoprotein A 1 in a sample. It is encoded in humans by the APOA1 gene and is the major protein component of high density lipoprotein (HDL) in plasma. Apolipoprotein A 1 relates to lower observational risk of coronary artery disease.

MONDO:0005649.title: appendicitis
MONDO:0005649.description: Acute inflammation of the vermiform appendix. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_0004517.title: arterial stiffness measurement
EFO_0004517.description: An Arterial stiffness measurement is the information output of a non invasive pulse wave velocity assay. Arterial stiffness is a predictor of cardiovascular mortality

MONDO:0005578.title: arthritis
MONDO:0005578.description: An inflammatory process affecting a joint. Causes include infection, autoimmune processes, degenerative processes, and trauma. Signs and symptoms may include swelling around the affected joint and pain. [NCIT: C2883]

EFO_0004736.title: aspartate aminotransferase measurement
EFO_0004736.description: Is a quantification of aspartate aminotransferase, and is commonly used as a marker for liver function.

EFO_0010934.title: aspartate aminotransferase to alanine aminotransferase ratio
EFO_0010934.description: The ratio between the levels of aspartate aminotransferase (AST) and alanine aminotransferase (ALT).

EFO_0007013.title: aspirin use measurement
EFO_0007013.description: quantification of some aspect of aspirin use

MONDO:0004980.title: atopic eczema
MONDO:0004980.description: A common chronic pruritic inflammatory skin disease with a strong genetic component. Onset typically occurs during the first 2 years of life. [OMIM: 603165]

MONDO:0004981.title: atrial fibrillation
MONDO:0004981.description: A disorder characterized by an electrocardiographic finding of a supraventricular arrhythmia characterized by the replacement of consistent P waves by rapid oscillations or fibrillatory waves that vary in size, shape and timing and are accompanied by an irregular ventricular response. (CDISC) [NCIT: P378]

MONDO:0005310.title: atrial flutter
MONDO:0005310.description: A disorder characterized by an electrocardiographic finding of an organized, regular atrial rhythm with atrial rate of 240-340 beats per minute. Multiple P waves typically appear in the inferior leads in a saw tooth-like pattern between the QRS complexes. (CDISC) [NCIT: P378]

MONDO:0007743.title: attention deficit hyperactivity disorder
MONDO:0007743.description: A disorder characterized by a marked pattern of inattention and/or hyperactivity-impulsivity that is inconsistent with developmental level and clearly interferes with functioning in at least two settings (e.g. at home and at school). When present, the symptoms of hyperactivity are most often present before the age of 7 years. There are three recognized presentations or subtypes from most to least common: combined type, inattentive/distractible type, hyperactive/impulsive type. [NCIT: C97160]

MONDO:0005258.title: autism spectrum disorder
MONDO:0005258.description: A spectrum of developmental disorders that includes autism, and Asperger syndrome. Signs and symptoms include poor communication skills, defective social interactions, and repetitive behaviors. [NCIT: P378]

HP_0003418.title: Back pain
HP_0003418.description: An unpleasant sensation characterized by physical discomfort (such as pricking, throbbing, or aching) localized to the back.

MONDO:0013662.title: Barrett's esophagus
MONDO:0013662.description: Esophageal lesion lined with columnar metaplastic epithelium which is flat or villiform. Barrett epithelium is characterized by two different types of cells: goblet cells and columnar cells. The symptomatology of Barrett esophagus is that of gastro-esophageal reflux. It is the precursor of most esophageal adenocarcinomas. (WHO) [NCIT: C2891]

MONDO:0020804.title: basal cell carcinoma
MONDO:0020804.description: A carcinoma involving the basal cells. [NCIT: C156767]

EFO_0007777.title: base metabolic rate measurement
EFO_0007777.description: quantification of an individual's base metabolic rate, the minimum amount of energy required to sustain life while at complete rest, including the body being in a post-absorptive state (ie the digestive system is inactive). BMR differs from RMR because BMR measurements must meet total physiological equilibrium whereas RMR conditions of measurement can be altered and defined by the contextual limitations.

EFO_0005090.title: basophil count
EFO_0005090.description: The number of granulocytes (polymorphonuclear leukocytes) in a specified volume of blood, usually 1 cubic millimeter. | quantification of basophils in the blood

EFO_0007992.title: basophil percentage of leukocytes
EFO_0007992.description: A calculated measurement in which the number of basophil granulocytes is divided by the number of all white blood cells in a specified sample of peripheral venous blood, and the result presented as a ratio, fraction, quotient or percentage. A basophil is a granular leukocyte with an irregularly shaped, relatively pale-staining nucleus that is partially constricted into two lobes, and with cytoplasm containing coarse granules of variable size that stain bluish-black when exposed to basic dyes.

EFO_0006946.title: behavioural disinhibition measurement
EFO_0006946.description: quantification of some aspect of behavioural disinhibition, including risky and impulsive behaviours. Behavioural disinhibition can be a marker for certain kinds of mental or behavioural disorders.

MONDO:0010811.title: benign prostatic hyperplasia
MONDO:0010811.description: A non-cancerous nodular enlargement of the prostate gland. It is characterized by the presence of epithelial cell nodules, and stromal nodules containing fibrous and smooth muscle elements. It is the most common urologic disorder in men, causing blockage of urine flow. [NCIT: C2897]

EFO_0004670.title: beta-amyloid 1-42 measurement
EFO_0004670.description: Is the quantification of Beta-amyloid 1-42 in cerebrospinal fluid typically used as a biomarker for Alzheimer's Disease

MONDO:0004868.title: biliary tract disease
MONDO:0004868.description: A disease involving the biliary tree.

EFO_0004570.title: bilirubin measurement
EFO_0004570.description: A bilirubin measurement is a quantification of bilirubin typically measured in serum.

EFO_0009963.title: bipolar I disorder
EFO_0009963.description: A bipolar disorder that is characterized by at least one manic or mixed episode.

EFO_0009964.title: bipolar II disorder
EFO_0009964.description: A bipolar disorder that is characterized by at least one hypomanic episode and at least one major depressive episode; with this disorder, depressive episodes are more frequent and more intense than manic episodes.

EFO_0004344.title: birth weight
EFO_0004344.description: The mass or quantity of heaviness of an individual at BIRTH. It is expressed by units of pounds or kilograms.

MONDO:0001531.title: blood coagulation disease
MONDO:0001531.description: A condition in which there is a deviation from or interruption of the normal coagulation properties of the blood. [NCIT: C2902]

OBA_VT0000188.title: blood glucose amount
OBA_VT0000188.description: The amount of a glucose when measured in blood. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute_location]

OBA_VT0002460.title: blood immunoglobulin amount
OBA_VT0002460.description: The amount of a immunoglobulin complex. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

OBA_VT0001560.title: blood insulin amount
OBA_VT0001560.description: The amount of a insulin (human) when measured in blood. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute_location]

OBA_VT0005416.title: blood protein amount
OBA_VT0005416.description: The amount of a protein when measured in blood. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute_location]

EFO_0008036.title: BMI-adjusted fasting blood glucose measurement
EFO_0008036.description: fasting blood glucose measurement that has been adjusted for subjects' body mass index

EFO_0008037.title: BMI-adjusted fasting blood insulin measurement
EFO_0008037.description: fasting insulin measurement that has been adjusted for subjects' body mass index

EFO_0007788.title: BMI-adjusted waist-hip ratio
EFO_0007788.description: waist-hip ratio that has been adjusted by subjects’ body mass index

EFO_0005106.title: body composition measurement
EFO_0005106.description: measurement of the percentages of fat, bone and muscle in human bodies

EFO_0004341.title: body fat distribution
EFO_0004341.description: Deposits of ADIPOSE TISSUE throughout the body. The pattern of fat deposits in the body regions is an indicator of health status. Excess ABDOMINAL FAT increases health risks more than excess fat around the hips or thighs, therefore, WAIST-HIP RATIO is often used to determine health risks.

EFO_0007800.title: body fat percentage
EFO_0007800.description: Quantification of the amount of body fat as a percentage of total body mass, calculated as either total mass of fat divided by total body mass or one of a number of more complex weighted equations. Body fat includes essential body fat and storage body fat.

OBA_VT0001253.title: body height
OBA_VT0001253.description: The height of a multicellular organism. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

EFO_0004340.title: body mass index
EFO_0004340.description: An indicator of body density as determined by the relationship of BODY WEIGHT to BODY HEIGHT. BMI=weight (kg)/height squared (m2). BMI correlates with body fat (ADIPOSE TISSUE). Their relationship varies with age and gender. For adults, BMI falls into these categories: below 18.5 (underweight); 18.5-24.9 (normal); 25.0-29.9 (overweight); 30.0 and above (obese). (National Center for Health Statistics, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention)

EFO_0004338.title: body weight
EFO_0004338.description: The mass or quantity of heaviness of an individual. It is expressed by units of pounds or kilograms.

EFO_0004324.title: body weights and measures
EFO_0004324.description: Measurements of the height, weight, length, area, etc., of the human and animal body or its parts.

EFO_0004514.title: bone quantitative ultrasound measurement
EFO_0004514.description: A bone quantitative ultrasound measurement is the output of a an ultrasound process in which bones are assayed using ultrasound and information about bone density, cortical thickness, elasticity and microarchitecture may be returned.

OBA_1000110.title: bone tissue density
OBA_1000110.description: The mass density of a bone tissue. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

OBA_2045221.title: bowel opening frequency
OBA_2045221.description: The frequency of a defecation rhythm. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

MONDO:0005291.title: brain aneurysm
MONDO:0005291.description: A congenital or acquired aneurysm within the cranium. [NCIT: P378]

MONDO:0021211.title: brain neoplasm
MONDO:0021211.description: A neoplasm (disease) that involves the brain. [MONDO: patterns/location]

OBA_2050021.title: brain stem volume
OBA_2050021.description: The volume of a brainstem. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

OBA_2050009.title: brain volume
OBA_2050009.description: The volume of a brain. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

MONDO:0004989.title: breast carcinoma
MONDO:0004989.description: A carcinoma that arises from epithelial cells of the breast [MONDO: DesignPattern]

MONDO:0001672.title: bronchus cancer
MONDO:0001672.description: A malignant neoplasm involving the bronchus

EFO_0004838.title: calcium measurement
EFO_0004838.description: Is a quantification of calcium, typically in serum. Calcium (Ca2+) plays a pivotal role in the physiology and biochemistry of organisms and the cell. It plays an important role in signal transduction pathways, where it acts as a second messenger, in neurotransmitter release from neurons, contraction of all muscle cell types, and fertilization. Many enzymes require calcium ions as a cofactor, those of the blood-clotting cascade being notable examples. Extracellular calcium is also important for maintaining the potential difference across excitable cell membranes, as well as proper bone formation.

MONDO:0004992.title: cancer
MONDO:0004992.description: A tumor composed of atypical neoplastic, often pleomorphic cells that invade other tissues. Malignant neoplasms often metastasize to distant anatomic sites and may recur after excision. The most common malignant neoplasms are carcinomas (adenocarcinomas or squamous cell carcinomas), Hodgkin and non-Hodgkin lymphomas, leukemias, melanomas, and sarcomas. [NCIT: C9305]

excluded_pgs.method: Clumped lung cancer loci (35 SNP score) combined with LASSO penalized regression (93 SNP score)

EFO_0010811.title: carbohydrate intake measurement
EFO_0010811.description: Quantification of the carbohydrate intake of an individual.

MONDO:0004994.title: cardiomyopathy
MONDO:0004994.description: A disease of the heart muscle or myocardium proper. Cardiomyopathies may be classified as either primary or secondary, on the basis of etiology, or on the pathophysiology of the lesion: hypertrophic, dilated, or restrictive. [NCIT: C34830]

MONDO:0004995.title: cardiovascular disease
MONDO:0004995.description: A disease involving the cardiovascular system.

excluded_pgs.method: Integrative PGS (also referred to as metaGRS) of 22 component PGSs
Integrative PGS (also referred to as metaGRS) of 22 component PGSs

EFO_0005278.title: cardiovascular disease biomarker measurement
EFO_0005278.description: cardiovascular disease biomarkers, such as ST2 cardiac biomarker and C-reactive protein, are used as indicators for cardiovascular disease and as predictors for therapeutic responses

OBA_2050108.title: carotid artery thickness
OBA_2050108.description: The thickness of a carotid artery segment. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

MONDO:0005129.title: cataract
MONDO:0005129.description: Partial or complete opacity of the crystalline lens of one or both eyes that decreases visual acuity and eventually results in blindness. Some cataracts appear in infancy or in childhood, but most develop in older individuals. (Sternberg Diagnostic Surgical Pathology, 3rd ed.) [NCIT: C26713]

EFO_0004830.title: caudate nucleus volume
EFO_0004830.description: The volume of the caudate nucleus, a brain structure implicated in many common neurological and psychiatric disorders

MONDO:0005230.title: cellulitis
MONDO:0005230.description: Inflammation of the dermis and subcutaneous tissues caused by a bacterial infection. Symptoms include erythema, edema, and pain to the affected area. [NCIT: C26715]

MONDO:0002714.title: central nervous system cancer
MONDO:0002714.description: A malignant neoplasm involving the central nervous system

OBA_VT0000254.title: cerebrospinal fluid amount
OBA_VT0000254.description: The volume of a cerebrospinal fluid. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

MONDO:0011057.title: cerebrovascular disorder
MONDO:0011057.description: A disorder resulting from inadequate blood flow in the vessels that supply the brain. Representative examples include cerebrovascular ischemia, cerebral embolism, and cerebral infarction. [NCIT: C2938]

excluded_pgs.method: Integrative PGS (also referred to as metaGRS) of 22 component PGSs
Integrative PGS (also referred to as metaGRS) of 22 component PGSs

MONDO:0005131.title: cervical carcinoma
MONDO:0005131.description: A carcinoma arising from either the exocervical squamous epithelium or the endocervical glandular epithelium. The major histologic types of cervical carcinoma are: squamous carcinoma, adenocarcinoma, adenosquamous carcinoma, adenoid cystic carcinoma and undifferentiated carcinoma. [NCIT: C9039]

HP_0100749.title: Chest pain
HP_0100749.description: An unpleasant sensation characterized by physical discomfort (such as pricking, throbbing, or aching) localized to the chest.

HP_0001082.title: Cholecystitis
HP_0001082.description: The presence of inflammatory changes in the gallbladder.

MONDO:0012672.title: cholelithiasis
MONDO:0012672.description: The presence of crystallized deposits forming in the gallbladder or biliary tree, primarily composed of cholesterol, bilirubin, and bile. [PMID: 29083691]

MONDO:0005300.title: chronic kidney disease
MONDO:0005300.description: Impairment of the renal function secondary to chronic kidney damage persisting for three or more months. [NCIT: C80078]

MONDO:0004948.title: chronic lymphocytic leukemia
MONDO:0004948.description: B-cell chronic lymphocytic leukemia (B-CLL) is a type of B-cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma, and the most common form of leukemia in Western countries, affecting elderly adults (mean age of 67 and 72 years) with a slight male predominance (1.7:1), and characterized by a highly variable clinical presentation that can include asymptomatic disease or non-specific B-symptoms such as unintentional weight loss, severe fatigue, fever (without evidence of infection), and night sweats as well as cervical lymphadenopathy, splenomegaly and frequent infections. Some patients can also develop autoimmune complications such as autoimmune hemolytic anemia or immune thrombocytopenia. The clinical course is extremely heterogeneous with survival ranging from a few months to several decades. [Orphanet: 67038]

MONDO:0005002.title: chronic obstructive pulmonary disease
MONDO:0005002.description: A chronic and progressive lung disorder characterized by the loss of elasticity of the bronchial tree and the air sacs, destruction of the air sacs wall, thickening of the bronchial wall, and mucous accumulation in the bronchial tree. The pathologic changes result in the disruption of the air flow in the bronchial airways. Signs and symptoms include shortness of breath, wheezing, productive cough, and chest tightness. The two main types of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease are chronic obstructive bronchitis and emphysema. [NCIT: C3199]

MONDO:0015129.title: chronic primary adrenal insufficiency
MONDO:0015129.description: A chronic disorder of the adrenal cortex resulting in the inadequate production of glucocorticoid and mineralocorticoid hormones. [Orphanet: 101959]

EFO_0008328.title: chronotype measurement
EFO_0008328.description: quantification of some aspect of chronotype such as ease of getting up in the morning

EFO_0006525.title: cigarettes per day measurement
EFO_0006525.description: the quantification of the number of cigarettes that a subject smoked on average per day

MONDO:0005155.title: cirrhosis of liver
MONDO:0005155.description: A disorder characterized by replacement of the liver parenchyma with fibrous tissue and regenerative nodules. It is usually caused by alcoholism, hepatitis B, and hepatitis C. Complications include the development of ascites, esophageal varices, bleeding, and hepatic encephalopathy. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_0003959.title: cleft lip
EFO_0003959.description: Congenital defect in the upper lip where the maxillary prominence fails to merge with the merged medial nasal prominences. It is thought to be caused by faulty migration of the mesoderm in the head region.

HP_0000175.title: Cleft palate
HP_0000175.description: Cleft palate is a developmental defect of the palate resulting from a failure of fusion of the palatine processes and manifesting as a separation of the roof of the mouth (soft and hard palate).

EFO_0000352.title: clinical history
EFO_0000352.description: Is an information entity about the material's (i.e., the patient's) medical record as background information relevant to the experiment.

EFO_0006781.title: coffee consumption measurement
EFO_0006781.description: the quantification of some coffee consumption or coffee consumption-related behaviour, usually self-reported via a questionnaire

EFO_0007710.title: cognitive decline measurement
EFO_0007710.description: quantification of some aspect of cognitive decline such as the rate at which it occurs or its severity, for example in patients with dementia or Alzheimer's disease. Cognitive decline is ususally assessed using a structured-interview protocol covering a number of standardised areas.

MONDO:0002032.title: colon carcinoma
MONDO:0002032.description: A carcinoma that arises from epithelial cells of the colon

HP_0002253.title: Colonic diverticula
HP_0002253.description: The presence of multiple diverticula of the colon.

MONDO:0024331.title: colorectal carcinoma
MONDO:0024331.description: A malignant epithelial neoplasm that arises from the colon or rectum and invades through the muscularis mucosa into the submucosa. The vast majority are adenocarcinomas. [NCIT: C2955]

MONDO:0001209.title: common wart
MONDO:0001209.description: A wart caused by human papillomavirus. It can appear anywhere on the skin. [NCIT: C27087]

EFO_0009819.title: comparative body size at age 10, self-reported
EFO_0009819.description: Description of an individual's body size at age 10 compared to average, as reported by the individual.

EFO_0009518.title: complication
EFO_0009518.description: Any disease or disorder that occurs during the course of, or because of, another disease, treatment, or procedure. [ NCI ]

MONDO:0015722.title: congenital vitamin K-dependent coagulation factors deficiency
MONDO:0015722.description: Congenital vitamin K-dependent coagulation factors deficiency involving multiple coagulation factors.

MONDO:0005009.title: congestive heart failure
MONDO:0005009.description: Failure of the heart to pump a sufficient amount of blood to meet the needs of the body tissues, resulting in tissue congestion and edema. Signs and symptoms include shortness of breath, pitting edema, enlarged tender liver, engorged neck veins, and pulmonary rales. [NCIT: C3080]

MONDO:0003900.title: connective tissue disease
MONDO:0003900.description: A disease involving the connective tissue.

MONDO:0000942.title: corneal disease
MONDO:0000942.description: A non-neoplastic or neoplastic disorder that affects the cornea. Representative examples include keratitis, bullous keratopathy, and squamous cell carcinoma. [NCIT: P378]

MONDO:0018102.title: corneal dystrophy
MONDO:0018102.description: The term corneal dystrophy embraces a heterogeneous group of bilateral genetically determined non-inflammatory corneal diseases that are usually restricted to the cornea. The designation is imprecise but remains in vogue because of its clinical value. [Orphanet: 34533]

EFO_0010066.title: corneal hysteresis
EFO_0010066.description: A measure of the biomechanical properties of the cornea. Low corneal hysteresis may be indicative of keratoconus.

EFO_0010067.title: corneal resistance factor
EFO_0010067.description: A measure of the biomechanical properties of the cornea. Low corneal resistance factor may be indicative of keratoconus.

MONDO:0005010.title: coronary artery disease
MONDO:0005010.description: Narrowing of the coronary arteries due to fatty deposits inside the arterial walls. The diagnostic criteria may include documented history of any of the following: documented coronary artery stenosis greater than or equal to 50% (by cardiac catheterization or other modality of direct imaging of the coronary arteries); previous coronary artery bypass surgery (CABG); previous percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI); previous myocardial infarction. (ACC) [NCIT: C26732]

MONDO:0100096.title: COVID-19
MONDO:0100096.description: A disease caused by infection with severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2. [MONDO: patterns/infectious_disease_by_agent]

EFO_0004458.title: C-reactive protein measurement
EFO_0004458.description: C-reactive protein (CRP) measurement is a measurement of the level of C-reactive protein in the blood.  Levels are known to rise in response to inflammation, CRP is therefore used as a clinical measure of inflammation. The measurement is used in the process of clinical diagnosis as high levels of CRP are associated with cardiovascular disease, diabetes and hypertension and in some cancers. | is about MESH:D002097

OBA_1001005.title: creatine level
OBA_1001005.description: The amount of a creatine when measured in anatomical entity. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute_location]

EFO_0007934.title: creatinine clearance measurement
EFO_0007934.description: The clearance rate of creatinine, that is, the volume of plasma that is cleared of creatinine by the kidneys per unit time. Creatinine clearance is calculated using the level of creatinine in a sample of urine, usually one collected over a period of 24 hours, the corresponding plasma creatinine level, and the volume of urine excreted. It is used as an approximation of the glomerular filtration rate (GFR).

MONDO:0005011.title: Crohn's disease
MONDO:0005011.description: A gastrointestinal disorder characterized by chronic inflammation involving all layers of the intestinal wall, noncaseating granulomas affecting the intestinal wall and regional lymph nodes, and transmural fibrosis. Crohn disease most commonly involves the terminal ileum; the colon is the second most common site of involvement. [NCIT: P378]

MONDO:0005012.title: cutaneous melanoma
MONDO:0005012.description: A primary melanoma arising from atypical melanocytes in the skin. Precursor lesions include acquired and congenital melanocytic nevi, and dysplastic nevi. Several histologic variants have been recognized, including superficial spreading melanoma, acral lentiginous melanoma, nodular melanoma, and lentigo maligna melanoma. [NCIT: C3510]

EFO_0004617.title: cystatin C measurement
EFO_0004617.description: is a quantification of serum cystatin C C (formerly gamma trace, post-gamma-globulin or neuroendocrine basic polypeptide),[1] a protein encoded by the CST3 gene, is mainly used as a biomarker of kidney function and kidney disease.

EFO_0001063.title: deafness
EFO_0001063.description: A partial or complete loss o f hearing in one or both ears; the level of impairment varies from a mild but important loss of sensitivity to a total loss of hearing. | An inherited or acquired condition characterized by a partial or complete loss of hearing in one or both ears. The level of impairment varies from a mild but important loss of sensitivity to a total loss of hearing. | An inherited or acquired condition characterized by the complete loss of the ability to hear from one or both ears.

EFO_0003907.title: deep vein thrombosis
EFO_0003907.description: The formation or presence of a blood clot (THROMBUS) within a deep vein in the lower extremity

OBA_2045237.title: dentate gyrus volume
OBA_2045237.description: The volume of a dentate gyrus of hippocampal formation. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

EFO_0010078.title: dentures
EFO_0010078.description: Dentures (also known as false teeth) are prosthetic devices constructed to replace missing teeth, and are supported by the surrounding soft and hard tissues of the oral cavity. Conventional dentures are removable (removable partial denture or complete denture). However, there are many denture designs, some which rely on bonding or clasping onto teeth or dental implants (fixed prosthodontics).

EFO_0007704.title: depressive episode measurement
EFO_0007704.description: quantification of some aspect, eg frequency or severity, of depressive episodes in patients with mental or behavioural disorders such as bipolar disorder or clinical depression

EFO_0007006.title: depressive symptom measurement
EFO_0007006.description: quantification of the existence and severity of depressive symptoms, usually via a questionnaire such as the Center for Epidemiological Studies Depression Scale (CES-D)

MONDO:0002406.title: dermatitis
MONDO:0002406.description: An inflammatory process affecting the skin. Signs include red rash, itching, and blister formation. Representative examples are contact dermatitis, atopic dermatitis, and seborrheic dermatitis. [NCIT: C2983]

EFO_0009519.title: device complication
EFO_0009519.description: Any problem arising from the use of a medical device. [ NCI ]

MONDO:0005015.title: diabetes mellitus
MONDO:0005015.description: A metabolic disorder characterized by abnormally high blood sugar levels due to diminished production of insulin or insulin resistance/desensitization. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_0009486.title: diabetic eye disease
EFO_0009486.description: A group of disorders affecting the eye in patients with diabetes mellitus. It includes cataract, glaucoma, retinopathy, and blindness. [ NCI ]

MONDO:0005266.title: diabetic retinopathy
MONDO:0005266.description: A chronic, pathological complication associated with diabetes mellitus, where retinal damages are incurred due to microaneurysms in the vasculature of the retina, progressively leading to abnormal blood vessel growth, and swelling and leaking of fluid from blood vessels, resulting in vision loss or blindness. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_0008561.title: diaphragmatic hernia
EFO_0008561.description: A congenital or acquired weakness or opening in the diaphragm which allows abdominal contents to protrude into the chest cavity; congenital diaphragmatic hernias are caused when the embryonic diaphragm fails to fuse. [ NCI ]

EFO_0006336.title: diastolic blood pressure
EFO_0006336.description: The blood pressure after the contraction of the heart while the chambers of the heart refill with blood.

EFO_0008111.title: diet measurement
EFO_0008111.description: quantification of some aspect of diet, including diet patterns, balance of nutrient consumption and glycemic load

MONDO:0018905.title: diffuse large B-cell lymphoma
MONDO:0018905.description: Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma is the most common subtype of non-Hodgkin lymphoma (NHL) in adults characterized by a median age of presentation in the sixth decade of life (but also rarely occurring in adolescents and children) with the initial presentation being single or multiple rapidly growing masses (that may or may not be painful) in nodal or extranodal sites (such as thyroid, skin, breast, gastrointestinal tract, testes, bone, or brain) and that can be accompanied by symptoms of fever, night sweats and weight loss. DLBCL has an aggressive disease course, with the elderly having a poorer prognosis than younger patients, and with relapses being common. [Orphanet: 544]

MONDO:0006181.title: Digestive System Carcinoma
MONDO:0006181.description: A malignant neoplasm that arises from the epithelium of any part of the digestive system. Representative examples include colorectal carcinoma, esophageal carcinoma, and pancreatic carcinoma. [NCIT: C96963]

MONDO:0004335.title: digestive system disease
MONDO:0004335.description: A disease or disorder that involves the digestive system. [MONDO: design_pattern]

MONDO:0005021.title: dilated cardiomyopathy
MONDO:0005021.description: Cardiomyopathy which is characterized by dilation and contractile dysfunction of the left and right ventricles. It may be idiopathic, or it may result from a myocardial infarction, myocardial infection, or alcohol abuse. It is a cause of congestive heart failure. [NCIT: P378]

MONDO:0056798.title: disorder of appendix
MONDO:0056798.description: A disease or disorder that involves the vermiform appendix. [MONDO: patterns/location]

MONDO:0002917.title: disorder of pilosebaceous unit
MONDO:0002917.description: A disease that involves the pilosebaceous unit. [MONDO: patterns/location]

MONDO:0004235.title: diverticulitis
MONDO:0004235.description: An infection that develops in the diverticula of the intestinal tract. Signs and symptoms include abdominal pain, fever, and leukocytosis. [NCIT: C26752]

EFO_0004315.title: drinking behavior
EFO_0004315.description: Behaviors associated with the ingesting of water and other liquids; includes rhythmic patterns of drinking (time intervals - onset and duration), frequency and satiety.

MONDO:0000775.title: drug allergy
MONDO:0000775.description: Immunologically mediated adverse reactions to medicinal substances used legally or illegally. [MESH: D004342]

EFO_0007010.title: drug use measurement
EFO_0007010.description: quantification of some aspect of drug use, such as whether drug use occured, over what period of time it occured and what dosage was used

MONDO:0005412.title: duodenal ulcer
MONDO:0005412.description: An ulcer in the duodenal wall. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_0004229.title: Dupuytren Contracture
EFO_0004229.description: A fibromatosis of the palmar fascia characterized by thickening and contracture of the fibrous bands on the palmar surfaces of the hand and fingers. It arises most commonly in men between the ages of 30 and 50.

EFO_0009817.title: ease of getting up in the morning, self-reported
EFO_0009817.description: Ease of getting up in the morning, as reported by an individual.

MONDO:0005451.title: eating disorder
MONDO:0005451.description: A broad group of psychological disorders with abnormal eating behaviors leading to physiological effects from overeating or insufficient food intake. [NCIT: C89332]

HP_0000964.title: Eczematoid dermatitis
HP_0000964.description: Eczema is a form of dermatitis that is characterized by scaly, pruritic, erythematous lesions located on flexural surfaces. [PMID: 32412211]

EFO_0011015.title: educational attainment
EFO_0011015.description: A measure of academic performance through educational benchmarks such as national exams and curriculum assessments.

EFO_0007803.title: emotional symptom measurement
EFO_0007803.description: quantification of some aspect of emotional problem symptoms, such as their existence and severity, usually via a standardised questionnaire such as the emotional symptoms subscale of the Strengths and Difficulties Questionnaire or interview with a qualified mental health professional

MONDO:0004849.title: emphysema
MONDO:0004849.description: A subcategory of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). It occurs in people who smoke and suffer from chronic bronchitis. It is characterized by inflation of the alveoli, alveolar wall damage, and reduction in the number of alveoli, resulting in difficulty breathing. [NCIT: C3348]

EFO_0009786.title: encounter with health service
EFO_0009786.description: Reason by which a person encounters a health service; useful to classify cases when the encounter is due to circumstances other than a disease or injury. This may happen (a) when a person who may or may not be sick encounters the health services for some specific purpose, such as to receive limited care or service for a current condition, to donate an organ or tissue, to receive prophylactic vaccination (immunization), or to discuss a problem which is in itself not a disease or injury; or (b) when some circumstance or problem is present which influences the person's health status but is not in itself a current illness or injury.

MONDO:0005151.title: endocrine system disease
MONDO:0005151.description: A disease involving the endocrine system.

MONDO:0011962.title: endometrial cancer
MONDO:0011962.description: Primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm involving the endometrium (mucous membrane that lines the endometrial cavity). [NCIT: C27815]

MONDO:0002447.title: endometrial carcinoma
MONDO:0002447.description: A malignant tumor arising from the epithelium that lines the cavity of the uterine body. The vast majority of endometrial carcinomas are adenocarcinomas; squamous cell and adenosquamous carcinomas represent a minority of the cases. Endometrioid adenocarcinoma is the most frequently seen variant of endometrial adenocarcinoma. Uterine bleeding is an initial clinical sign. The prognosis depends on the stage of the tumor, the depth of myometrial wall invasion, and the degree of differentiation. [NCIT: C7558]

MONDO:0005133.title: endometriosis
MONDO:0005133.description: The growth of functional endometrial tissue in anatomic sites outside the uterine body. It most often occurs in the pelvic organs. [NCIT: C3014]

MONDO:0019437.title: enthesitis-related juvenile idiopathic arthritis
MONDO:0019437.description: Enthesitis-related juvenile idiopathic arthritis is a subtype of juvenile idiopathic arthritis that is characterized by both arthritis and inflammation of an enthesitis site (the point at which a ligament, tendon, or joint capsule attaches to the bone). Signs and symptoms generally develop in late childhood or early adolescence and include pain, tenderness, and swelling in joints and at the enthesis. The knee and the back of the ankle (at the Achilles tendon) are the most commonly affected parts of the body. The underlying cause of enthesitis-related juvenile idiopathic arthritis is currently unknown (idiopathic). It is very rare for more than one member of a family to have juvenile arthritis; however, research suggests that having a family member with juvenile arthritis or any autoimmune disease may increase the risk of having juvenile arthritis, in general. Treatment usually involves different types of medications to help manage symptoms and/or physical therapy.

EFO_0004842.title: eosinophil count
EFO_0004842.description: Is a quantification of eosinphils in blood. | The number of granulocytes (polymorphonuclear leukocytes) in a specified volume of blood, usually 1 cubic millimeter.

EFO_0007991.title: eosinophil percentage of leukocytes
EFO_0007991.description: A calculated measurement in which the number of eosinophil granulocytes is divided by the number of all white blood cells in a specified sample of peripheral venous blood, and the result presented as a ratio, fraction, quotient or percentage. An eosinophil is a granular leukocyte having a nucleus with two lobes connected by a thread of chromatin, and cytoplasm containing coarse, round granules of uniform size that stain readily with eosin and other acid dyes.

MONDO:0005027.title: epilepsy
MONDO:0005027.description: A brain disorder characterized by episodes of abnormally increased neuronal discharge resulting in transient episodes of sensory or motor neurological dysfunction, or psychic dysfunction. These episodes may or may not be associated with loss of consciousness or convulsions. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_1001317.title: Epiphyses, Slipped
EFO_1001317.description: One of the most common adolescent hip disorders and represents a unique type of instability of the proximal femoral growth plate. Four separate clinical groups are seen: Pre-slip (wide epiphyseal line without slippage), acute form (10-15% - slippage occurs suddenly, normally spontaneous), acute-on-chronic (slippage occurs acutely where there is already existing chronic slip) and chronic (85% - steadily progressive slippage, the most common form).

EFO_0009271.title: Epstein Barr virus nuclear antigen-1 seropositivity
EFO_0009271.description: The result of a measurement of circulating Epstein Barr virus nuclear antigen-1 antibodies used in the diagnosis of latent Epstein-Barr virus infection

EFO_0004305.title: erythrocyte count
EFO_0004305.description: The number of red blood cells per unit volume in a sample of venous blood.

OBA_0003460.title: erythrocyte volume
OBA_0003460.description: The volume of a erythrocyte. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

MONDO:0005028.title: esophageal adenocarcinoma
MONDO:0005028.description: A malignant tumor with glandular differentiation arising predominantly from Barrett mucosa in the lower third of the esophagus. Rare examples of esophageal adenocarcinoma deriving from ectopic gastric mucosa in the upper esophagus have also been reported. Grossly, esophageal adenocarcinomas are similar to esophageal squamous cell carcinomas. Microscopically, adenocarcinomas arising in the setting of Barrett esophagus are typically papillary and/or tubular. The prognosis is poor. [NCIT: C4025]

MONDO:0007576.title: esophageal cancer
MONDO:0007576.description: A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm involving the esophagus. [NCIT: C7478]

MONDO:0019086.title: esophageal carcinoma
MONDO:0019086.description: Esophageal carcinoma (EC) is a tumor arising in the epithelial cells lining the esophagus and can be divided into two subtypes: esophageal squamous cell carcinoma (ESCC) and esophageal adenocarcinoma (EAC). [Orphanet: 70482]

MONDO:0003749.title: esophageal disease
MONDO:0003749.description: A non-neoplastic or neoplastic disorder that affects the esophagus. Representative examples of non-neoplastic disorders include esophagitis and esophageal ulcer. Representative examples of neoplastic disorders include carcinomas, lymphomas, and melanomas. [NCIT: C3027]

MONDO:0006513.title: estrogen-receptor negative breast cancer
MONDO:0006513.description: A subtype of breast cancer that is estrogen-receptor negative [EFO: 1000650]

MONDO:0006512.title: estrogen-receptor positive breast cancer
MONDO:0006512.description: A subtype of breast cancer that is estrogen-receptor positive [EFO: 1000649]

EFO_0009332.title: executive function measurement
EFO_0009332.description: Quantification of executive function in an individual, for example using the Behavioural Rating Inventory of Executive Function (BRIEF). Tests of executive function may be performed as part of a more comprehensive assessment to diagnose neurological and psychiatric disorders.

MONDO:0005328.title: eye disease
MONDO:0005328.description: A non-neoplastic or neoplastic disorder that affects the eye. Representative examples include conjunctivitis, glaucoma, cataract, conjunctival squamous cell carcinoma, uveal melanoma, and retinoblastoma. [NCIT: C26767]

EFO_0004731.title: eye measurement
EFO_0004731.description: Is a quantification of some eye structure or property of an eye structure, such as corneal thickness.

EFO_0004630.title: factor VIII measurement
EFO_0004630.description: Is a quantification of factor VIII in serum, Factor VIII (FVIII) is an essential blood clotting protein, also known as anti-hemophilic factor (AHF). In humans, Factor VIII is encoded by the F8 gene.[1][2] Defects in this gene results in hemophilia A.

EFO_0009631.title: fall
EFO_0009631.description: A sudden movement downward, usually resulting in injury. [ NCI ]

EFO_0000493.title: family history
EFO_0000493.description: Family history is a form of clinicaly history specifically about relevant aspects of genetic preconditions or family member's clinical history.

EFO_0009268.title: family history of Alzheimer’s disease
EFO_0009268.description: A reported family history of Alzheimer’s disease in one or more family members.

EFO_0005606.title: family history of breast cancer
EFO_0005606.description: A reported family history of breast cancer in one or more family members.

EFO_0006953.title: family history of lung cancer
EFO_0006953.description: A reported family history of lung cancer in one or more family members.

EFO_0600083.title: family history of prostate cancer
EFO_0600083.description: A reported family history of prostate cancer in one or more family members.

EFO_0009712.title: father's age at death
EFO_0009712.description: The age of a person's father at which father's death occurred.

OBA_0004528.title: fat pad mass
OBA_0004528.description: The mass of a fat pad. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

EFO_0009599.title: feeling emotionally hurt measurement
EFO_0009599.description: Quantification of whether an individual feels or felt emotionally hurt.

EFO_0009597.title: feeling nervous measurement
EFO_0009597.description: Quantification of whether an individual feels or felt nervous or "suffered from nerves".

PATO_0000383.title: female
PATO_0000383.description: A biological sex quality inhering in an individual or a population that only produces gametes that can be fertilised by male gametes.

excluded_pgs.method: Composite PRS (component scores combined by log(HR))

MONDO:0021148.title: female reproductive system neoplasm
MONDO:0021148.description: A benign, precancerous, or malignant neoplasm that affects the female reproductive system. Representative examples include uterine corpus leiomyoma, endocervical polyp, ovarian carcinoma, cervical carcinoma, and endometrial carcinoma. [NCIT: C3053]

EFO_0004459.title: ferritin measurement
EFO_0004459.description: A ferritin measurement a is measurement of ferritin level  in serum as an indicator of iron metabolism

EFO_0010139.title: fish consumption measurement
EFO_0010139.description: Quantification of some fish consumption or fish consumption-related behaviour, usually self-reported via a questionnaire.

OBA_1001015.title: folic acid level
OBA_1001015.description: The amount of a folic acid when measured in anatomical entity. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute_location]

EFO_1001329.title: Follicular Cyst
EFO_1001329.description: Cyst due to the occlusion of the duct of a follicle or small gland.

EFO_0004314.title: forced expiratory volume
EFO_0004314.description: Measure of the maximum amount of air that can be expelled in a given number of seconds during aÂ FORCED VITAL CAPACITYÂ determination . It is usually given asÂ FEVÂ followed by a subscript indicating the number of seconds over which the measurement is made, although it is sometimes given as a percentage of forced vital capacity.

OBA_2050047.title: fourth ventricle volume
OBA_2050047.description: The volume of a fourth ventricle. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

EFO_0009885.title: frailty measurement
EFO_0009885.description: Quantification of some aspect of frailty.  Frailty is usually described as clinical state of heightened vulnerability to poor resolution of homeostasis after a stressor event, which thereby increases the risk of adverse outcomes, including falls, delirium, disability and mortality.

MONDO:0005346.title: gallstones
MONDO:0005346.description: Solid crystalline precipitates in the biliary tract, usually formed in the gallbladder, resulting in the condition of cholelithiasis. Gallstones, derived from the bile, consist mainly of calcium, cholesterol, or bilirubin. [MESH: D042882]

MONDO:0007186.title: gastroesophageal reflux disease
MONDO:0007186.description: A chronic disorder characterized by reflux of the gastric and/or duodenal contents into the distal esophagus. It is usually caused by incompetence of the lower esophageal sphincter. Symptoms include heartburn and acid indigestion. It may cause injury to the esophageal mucosa. [NCIT: C26781]

EFO_0005112.title: gestational age
EFO_0005112.description: The age of the conceptus, beginning from the time of FERTILIZATION. In clinical obstetrics, the gestational age is often estimated as the time from the last day of the last MENSTRUATION which is about 2 weeks before OVULATION and fertilization.

MONDO:0005406.title: gestational diabetes
MONDO:0005406.description: Carbohydrate intolerance first diagnosed during pregnancy. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_0006829.title: GFR change measurement
EFO_0006829.description: A quantification of the variation in an individual's glomerular filtration rate (GFR) over a period of time used in assessment of kidney function and diagnosis of kidney disease.

MONDO:0021042.title: glioma
MONDO:0021042.description: A benign or malignant brain and spinal cord tumor that arises from glial cells (astrocytes, oligodendrocytes, ependymal cells). Tumors that arise from astrocytes are called astrocytic tumors or astrocytomas. Tumors that arise from oligodendrocytes are called oligodendroglial tumors. Tumors that arise from ependymal cells are called ependymomas. [NCIT: C3059]

OBA_0003747.title: glomerular filtration rate
OBA_0003747.description: The rate of a glomerular filtration. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

EFO_0004307.title: glucose tolerance test
EFO_0004307.description: Quantification of the ability of an individual to maintain homeostasis of blood glucose, via a test procedure including measuring blood glucose levels in a fasting state, and at prescribed intervals before and after oral glucose intake (75 or 100 g) or intravenous infusion (0.5 g/kg).

MONDO:0005393.title: gout
MONDO:0005393.description: A condition characterized by painful swelling of the joints, which is caused by deposition of urate crystals. [NCIT: C34650]

EFO_0005420.title: grey matter volume measurement
EFO_0005420.description: quantification of the volume of grey matter in the brain, usuallly through an MRI scan. Grey matter volume is associated with schizophrenia.

EFO_0006941.title: grip strength measurement
EFO_0006941.description: quantification of the force applied by the hand to pull on or suspend from objects

HP_0001822.title: Hallux valgus
HP_0001822.description: Lateral deviation of the great toe (i.e., in the direction of the little toe).

EFO_0009902.title: handedness
EFO_0009902.description: A better, faster, or more precise performance or individual preference for use of a hand, known as the dominant hand; the less capable or less preferred hand is called the non-dominant hand. Dominance typically applies to all tasks, but in rare cases may be task-specific (known as cross-handedness or mixed-dominance) Ambidexterity is the ability to do any task equally well with either hand.

EFO_0004541.title: HbA1c measurement
EFO_0004541.description: A quantification of glycated A1c hemoglobin in blood used as an index for blood glucose level over several months.

HP_0002315.title: Headache
HP_0002315.description: Cephalgia, or pain sensed in various parts of the head, not confined to the area of distribution of any nerve. [PMID: 15304572]

MONDO:0021146.title: headache disorder
MONDO:0021146.description: Various conditions with the symptom of headache. Headache disorders are classified into major groups, such as primary headache disorders (based on characteristics of their headache symptoms) and secondary headache disorders (based on their etiologies). (International Classification of Headache Disorders, 2nd ed. Cephalalgia 2004: suppl 1) [MESH: D020773]

MONDO:0010150.title: head and neck squamous cell carcinoma
MONDO:0010150.description: A squamous cell carcinoma that arises from any of the following anatomic sites: lip and oral cavity, nasal cavity, paranasal sinuses, pharynx, larynx, and salivary glands. [NCIT: C34447]

EFO_0011014.title: health-related quality of life measurement
EFO_0011014.description: A measure to assess health-related quality of life (HRQOL), which domains include physical health, emotional health, cognitive functioning, fatigue and pain.

EFO_0007652.title: health trait
EFO_0007652.description: The characteristics in health that a certain person has.

MONDO:0021945.title: hearing disorder
MONDO:0021945.description: A disorder characterized by the partial or complete loss of the ability to detect sounds due to damage to the ear structures or inability of the brain to properly interpret or process the auditory signals it receives from the anatomic structures of the ear. [NCIT: C3078]

MONDO:0005365.title: hearing loss
MONDO:0005365.description: A partial or complete loss of hearing in one or both ears. It is classified as conductive, sensory, or central. [NCIT: C35731]

MONDO:0005267.title: heart disease
MONDO:0005267.description: A disease involving the heart and/or pericardium.

MONDO:0005252.title: heart failure
MONDO:0005252.description: Inability of the heart to pump blood at an adequate rate to meet tissue metabolic requirements. Clinical symptoms of heart failure include: unusual dyspnea on light exertion, recurrent dyspnea occurring in the supine position, fluid retention or rales, jugular venous distension, pulmonary edema on physical exam, or pulmonary edema on chest x-ray presumed to be cardiac dysfunction. [EFO: 0003144]

OBA_1001087.title: heart rate
OBA_1001087.description: The rate of a heart contraction. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

EFO_0009270.title: heel bone mineral density
EFO_0009270.description: Quantification of the mineral density of the heel bone

EFO_0004348.title: hematocrit
EFO_0004348.description: The volume of packed RED BLOOD CELLS in a blood specimen. The volume is measured by centrifugation in a tube with graduated markings, or with automated blood cell counters. It is an indicator of erythrocyte status in disease. For example, ANEMIA shows a low value; POLYCYTHEMIA, a high value.

EFO_0004503.title: hematological measurement
EFO_0004503.description: A measurement quantifying some blood cell, or component.

MONDO:0005570.title: hematologic disease
MONDO:0005570.description: A disease involving the hematopoietic system.

HP_0000790.title: Hematuria
HP_0000790.description: The presence of blood in the urine. Hematuria may be gross hematuria (visible to the naked eye) or microscopic hematuria (detected by dipstick or microscopic examination of the urine).

EFO_0004509.title: hemoglobin measurement
EFO_0004509.description: Hemoglobin measurement is a measure of the quantity of the metallo protein hemoglobin in blood often used in the diagnosis of anaemia.

MONDO:0004872.title: hemorrhoid
MONDO:0004872.description: Dilated veins in the anal canal. [NCIT: C26792]

MONDO:0000618.title: HER2 negative breast carcinoma
MONDO:0000618.description: A biologic subset of breast carcinoma defined by absence of HER2.

MONDO:0006244.title: HER2 Positive Breast Carcinoma
MONDO:0006244.description: A biologic subset of breast carcinoma defined by high expression of HER2, GRB7, and TRAP100, and by lack of expression of estrogen receptor (ER). [NCIT: C53556]

MONDO:0005609.title: Herpes Zoster
MONDO:0005609.description: A common dermal and neurologic disorder caused by reactivation of the varicella-zoster virus that has remained dormant within dorsal root ganglia, often for decades, after the patient's initial exposure to the virus in the form of varicella (chickenpox). It is characterized by severe neuralgic pain along the distribution of the affected nerve and crops of clustered vesicles over the area. [NCIT: C71079]

HP_0002036.title: Hiatus hernia
HP_0002036.description: The presence of a hernia in which the upper part of the stomach, i.e., mainly the gastric cardia protrudes through the diaphragmatic esophageal hiatus.

OBA_1000032.title: hip circumference
OBA_1000032.description: The circumference of a hip. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

EFO_0009401.title: hippocampal amigdala transition area volume
EFO_0009401.description: Volume of hippocampal amigdala transition area.

EFO_0009394.title: hippocampal CA1 volume
EFO_0009394.description: Volume of hippocampal CA1, which is the part of hippocampus proper bounded by CA2 and the subiculum, characterized by pyramidal neurons that receive projections from pyramidal neurons of CA3 via the Schaffer collaterals.

EFO_0009395.title: hippocampal CA3 volume
EFO_0009395.description: Volume of hippocampal CA3, which is the part of hippocampus proper bounded by the hilus of the dentate gyrus and area CA2, characterized by large pyramidal cells and a dense projection from dentate gyrus granule cell mossy fibers.

OBA_2050043.title: hippocampal fissure volume
OBA_2050043.description: The volume of a hippocampal sulcus. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

OBA_2045244.title: hippocampal formation volume
OBA_2045244.description: The volume of a hippocampal formation. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

EFO_0005035.title: hippocampal volume
EFO_0005035.description: Measurement of the volume of the hippocampus

OBA_2045245.title: hippocampus fimbria volume
OBA_2045245.description: The volume of a hippocampus fimbria. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

OBA_2050050.title: hippocampus molecular layer volume
OBA_2050050.description: The volume of a hippocampus molecular layer. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

MONDO:0004952.title: Hodgkins lymphoma
MONDO:0004952.description: A heterogeneous group of malignant lymphoid neoplasms of B-cell origin characterized histologically by the presence of Hodgkin and Reed-Sternberg (HRS) cells in the vast majority of cases. There are two distinct subtypes: nodular lymphocyte predominant Hodgkin lymphoma and classical Hodgkin lymphoma. Hodgkin lymphoma involves primarily lymph nodes. [NCIT: C9357] [Orphanet: 98293]

EFO_0004469.title: HOMA-B
EFO_0004469.description: The HOMA-B measurement employs the homeostatic model assessment (HOMA)  to quantify beta-cell function.

EFO_0004501.title: HOMA-IR
EFO_0004501.description: The HOMA-IR measurement employs the homeostatic model assessment (HOMA)  to quantify insulin resistance.

EFO_0009695.title: household income
EFO_0009695.description: A demographic parameter indicating the amount of earning made by a family.

HP_0003124.title: Hypercholesterolemia
HP_0003124.description: An increased concentration of cholesterol in the blood. [HPO_CONTRIBUTOR: gcarletti]

MONDO:0004425.title: hyperthyroidism
MONDO:0004425.description: Overactivity of the thyroid gland resulting in overproduction of thyroid hormone and increased metabolic rate. Causes include diffuse hyperplasia of the thyroid gland (Graves' disease), single nodule in the thyroid gland, and thyroiditis. The symptoms are related to the increased metabolic rate and include weight loss, fatigue, heat intolerance, excessive sweating, diarrhea, tachycardia, insomnia, muscle weakness, and tremor. [NCIT: P378]

MONDO:0005045.title: hypertrophic cardiomyopathy
MONDO:0005045.description: A condition in which the myocardium is hypertrophied without an obvious cause. The hypertrophy is generally asymmetric and may be associated with obstruction of the ventricular outflow tract. [NCIT: C34449]

HP_0001943.title: Hypoglycemia
HP_0001943.description: A decreased concentration of glucose in the blood.

MONDO:0005420.title: hypothyroidism
MONDO:0005420.description: Abnormally low levels of thyroid hormone. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_0004194.title: IGA glomerulonephritis
EFO_0004194.description: Inflammation of a specific segment of glomeruli within the kidney. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_0004627.title: IGF-1 measurement
EFO_0004627.description: Is the quantification of Insulin-like growth factor 1 (IGF-1), also called somatomedin C. IGF-1 is a hormone similar in molecular structure to insulin. It plays an important role in childhood growth and continues to have anabolic effects in adults. Levels of IGF-1 are known to increase in some cancers.

EFO_0020464.title: immunoglobulin E measurement
EFO_0020464.description: The determination of the amount of immunoglobulin E in a sample

HP_0032263.title: Increased blood pressure
HP_0032263.description: Abnormal increase in blood pressure. An individual measurement of increased blood pressure does not necessarily imply hypertension. In practical terms, multiple measurements are recommended to diagnose the presence of hypertension. [PMID: 9137951]

MONDO:0005265.title: inflammatory bowel disease
MONDO:0005265.description: A spectrum of small and large bowel inflammatory diseases of unknown etiology. It includes Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis, and colitis of indeterminate type. [NCIT: C3138]
