HP_0000023.title: Inguinal hernia
HP_0000023.description: Protrusion of the contents of the abdominal cavity through the inguinal canal.

MONDO:0013600.title: insomnia
MONDO:0013600.description: A sleep disorder characterized by difficulty in falling asleep and/or remaining asleep. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_0007876.title: insomnia measurement
EFO_0007876.description: quantification of insomnia such as how often it occurs and its severity

OBA_2050130.title: insulin-like protein level
OBA_2050130.description: The amount of a insulin-like protein when measured in anatomical entity. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute_location]

HP_0000855.title: Insulin resistance
HP_0000855.description: Increased resistance towards insulin, that is, diminished effectiveness of insulin in reducing blood glucose levels.

EFO_0008473.title: insulin response measurement
EFO_0008473.description: Quantification of some aspect of insulin response, typically measuring insulin levels in the blood following a glucose challenge

OBA_VT0003564.title: insulin secretion trait
OBA_VT0003564.description: The quality of a insulin secretion. [obo: oba/patterns/entity_attribute_part_of]

EFO_0004471.title: insulin sensitivity measurement
EFO_0004471.description: An insulin sensitivity measurement is a measurement of insulin metabolism, which can be measured in a number of ways, for example using the HOMA model or via a glucose tolerance test.

EFO_0600082.title: insulin use measurement
EFO_0600082.description: Quantification of some aspect of the use of insulin.

EFO_0004337.title: intelligence
EFO_0004337.description: The ability to learn and to deal with new situations and to deal effectively with tasks involving abstractions.

MONDO:0015925.title: interstitial lung disease
MONDO:0015925.description: A diverse group of lung diseases that affect the lung parenchyma. They are characterized by an initial inflammation of pulmonary alveoli that extends to the interstitium and beyond leading to diffuse pulmonary fibrosis. Interstitial lung diseases are classified by their etiology (known or unknown causes), and radiological-pathological features. [MESH: D017563]

EFO_1001800.title: Intervertebral Disc Displacement
EFO_1001800.description: An INTERVERTEBRAL DISC in which the nucleus pulposus has protruded through surrounding fibrocartilage. This occurs most frequently in the lower lumbar region.

MONDO:0005020.title: intestinal disease
MONDO:0005020.description: A non-neoplastic or neoplastic disorder that affects the small or large intestine. [NCIT: C26801]

MONDO:0013792.title: intracerebral hemorrhage
MONDO:0013792.description: A cerebrovascular disorder characterized by bleeding into one or both cerebral hemispheres including the basal ganglia and the cerebral cortex. It is often associated with hypertension and craniocerebral trauma. [MESH: D002543] [PMID: 31971743]

EFO_0004695.title: intraocular pressure measurement
EFO_0004695.description: Is a quantification of intraocular pressure. Increased intraocular pressure is a heritable risk factor for primary open angle glaucoma.

HP_0001094.title: Iridocyclitis
HP_0001094.description: A type of anterior uveitis, in which there is Inflammation of the iris and the ciliary body.

MONDO:0006816.title: joint disease
MONDO:0006816.description: Any disorder of the joints. [NCIT: C35760]

MONDO:0011429.title: juvenile idiopathic arthritis
MONDO:0011429.description: Juvenile idiopathic arthritis (JIA) is the term used to describe a group of inflammatory articular disorders of unknown cause that begin before the age of 16 and last over 6 weeks. The term juvenile idiopathic arthritis was chosen to signify the absence of any known mechanism underlying the disorder and to highlight the necessity of excluding other types of arthritis occurring in well defined diseases (in particular arthritis occurring in association with infectious, inflammatory and haematooncologic diseases). [Orphanet: 92]

MONDO:0015486.title: keratoconus
MONDO:0015486.description: A degenerative, structural disorder of the eye, characterized by a cone-shaped protrusion of the cornea. It may lead to visual disturbances. [NCIT: P378]

MONDO:0002367.title: kidney cancer
MONDO:0002367.description: Primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm involving the kidney. [NCIT: C7548]

MONDO:0001106.title: kidney failure
MONDO:0001106.description: An acute or chronic condition that is characterized by the inability of the kidneys to adequately filter the blood. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_1001870.title: late-onset Alzheimer's disease
EFO_1001870.description: This is the most common form of the disease, which happens to people age 65 and older. It may or may not run in families. So far, researchers haven’t found a particular gene that causes it. No one knows for sure why some people get it and others don’t.

EFO_0004995.title: lean body mass
EFO_0004995.description: The weight of all organs and tissue in an individual less the weight of the individual's body fat.

EFO_0008373.title: left ventricular ejection fraction measurement
EFO_0008373.description: quantification of the volumetric fraction of blood pumped out of the left ventricle

EFO_0009290.title: left ventricular mass index
EFO_0009290.description: The left ventricular mass divided by the subject's body surface area

EFO_0010555.title: left ventricular stroke volume measurement
EFO_0010555.description: Quantification of the volume of blood pumped from the left ventricle of the heart with each beat, calculated by subtracting the volume of blood in the ventricle at the end of a beat (end-systolic volume) from the volume of blood just prior to the beat (end-diastolic volume).

EFO_0008206.title: left ventricular systolic function measurement
EFO_0008206.description: quantification of some aspect of the systolic function of the left cardiac ventricle such as ventricular fractional shortening or systolic dysfunction

OBA_VT0000217.title: leukocyte quantity
OBA_VT0000217.description: The amount of a leukocyte. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

OBA_2045206.title: level of serum globulin type protein
OBA_2045206.description: The amount of a globulin type when measured in blood serum. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute_location]

OBA_VT0005372.title: life span determination trait
OBA_VT0005372.description: The process quality of a determination of adult lifespan. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

EFO_0010724.title: lifestyle measurement
EFO_0010724.description: Quantification of some aspect of a subject's lifestyle.

EFO_0010821.title: liver fat measurement
EFO_0010821.description: A quantification of the fat content of the liver such as liver fat fraction, typically assessed by MRI.

EFO_0007865.title: loneliness measurement
EFO_0007865.description: Quantification of some aspect of loneliness, such as severity or presence/absence, generally through administration of a standardised questionnaire such as the 3-Item Leave Behind Questionnaire (LBQ).

EFO_0007645.title: longitudinal alcohol consumption measurement
EFO_0007645.description: quantification of some aspect of alcohol consumption over a long period of time (typically several years)

EFO_0004611.title: low density lipoprotein cholesterol measurement
EFO_0004611.description: The measurement of LDL cholesterol in blood used as a risk indicator for heart disease.

MONDO:0005061.title: lung adenocarcinoma
MONDO:0005061.description: A carcinoma that arises from the lung and is characterized by the presence of malignant glandular epithelial cells. There is a male predilection with a male to female ratio of 2:1. Usually lung adenocarcinoma is asymptomatic and is identified through screening studies or as an incidental radiologic finding. If clinical symptoms are present they include shortness of breath, cough, hemoptysis, chest pain, and fever. Tobacco smoke is a known risk factor. [NCIT: C3512]

MONDO:0008903.title: lung cancer
MONDO:0008903.description: A malignant neoplasm involving the lung.

excluded_pgs.method: Clumped lung cancer loci (35 SNP score) combined with LASSO penalized regression (93 SNP score)

MONDO:0005138.title: lung carcinoma
MONDO:0005138.description: A carcinoma that arises from epithelial cells of the lung [MONDO: DesignPattern]

excluded_pgs.method: Clumped lung cancer loci (35 SNP score) combined with LASSO penalized regression (93 SNP score)

EFO_0004587.title: lymphocyte count
EFO_0004587.description: A quantification of lymphocytes in blood.

EFO_0007993.title: lymphocyte percentage of leukocytes
EFO_0007993.description: A calculated measurement in which the number of lymphocytes in a specified sample of blood is divided by the total number of white blood cells in the sample, and the result presented as a ratio, fraction, quotient or percentage.

MONDO:0005402.title: lymphoid leukemia
MONDO:0005402.description: A malignant lymphocytic neoplasm of B-cell or T-cell lineage involving primarily the bone marrow and the peripheral blood. This category includes precursor or acute lymphoblastic leukemias and chronic leukemias. [NCIT: C7539]

MONDO:0003004.title: macular degeneration
MONDO:0003004.description: Loss of vision in the central portion of the retina (macula), secondary to retinal degeneration. [NCIT: P378]

MONDO:0002009.title: major depressive disorder
MONDO:0002009.description: An episode of depression lasting two or more weeks without an intervening episode of mania. [NCIT: P378]

HP_0002024.title: Malabsorption
HP_0002024.description: Impaired ability to absorb one or more nutrients from the intestine.

PATO_0000384.title: male
PATO_0000384.description: A biological sex quality inhering in an individual or a population whose sex organs contain only male gametes.

excluded_pgs.method: Composite PRS (component scores combined by log(HR))

MONDO:0005836.title: male reproductive organ cancer
MONDO:0005836.description: A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm involving the male reproductive system. Representative examples include prostate carcinoma, penile carcinoma, testicular seminoma, and testicular embryonal carcinoma. [NCIT: C8561]

MONDO:0002352.title: Malignant Laryngeal Neoplasm
MONDO:0002352.description: A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm involving the larynx. The majority are carcinomas. [NCIT: C7484]

MONDO:0017604.title: marginal zone B-cell lymphoma
MONDO:0017604.description: A usually indolent mature B-cell lymphoma, arising from the marginal zone of lymphoid tissues. It is characterized by the presence of small to medium sized atypical lymphocytes. It comprises three entities, according to the anatomic sites involved: extranodal marginal zone B-cell lymphoma of mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue, which affects extranodal sites (most often stomach, lung, skin, and ocular adnexa); nodal marginal zone B-cell lymphoma, which affects lymph nodes without evidence of extranodal disease; and splenic marginal zone B-cell lymphoma, which affects the spleen and splenic hilar lymph nodes, bone marrow, and often the peripheral blood. [NCIT: C4341]

EFO_0006340.title: mean arterial pressure
EFO_0006340.description: The mean pressure of the blood within the arterial circulation. The arterial pressure may be directly measured by insertion of an intra-arterial catheter connected to a transducer. The mean arterial pressure (MAP) can be calculated by subsequent analysis of the waveform. MAP can be approximated without an invasive procedure using the following formula: diastolic pressure plus 1/3 of the pulse pressure, where pulse pressure is systolic pressure - diastolic pressure.

EFO_0004527.title: mean corpuscular hemoglobin
EFO_0004527.description: The MCH is  the average mass of hemoglobin per red blood cell in a sample of blood and is calculated by dividing the total mass of hemoglobin by the RBC count

EFO_0004528.title: mean corpuscular hemoglobin concentration
EFO_0004528.description: The mean corpuscular hemoglobin concentration is a measure of the concentration of hemoglobin in a given volume of packed red blood cell

EFO_0002571.title: medical procedure
EFO_0002571.description: An activity that produces an effect, or that is intended to alter the course of a disease in a patient or population. This is a general term that encompasses the medical, social, behavioral, and environmental acts that can have preventive, therapeutic, or palliative effects.

MONDO:0005105.title: melanoma
MONDO:0005105.description: A malignant, usually aggressive tumor composed of atypical, neoplastic melanocytes. Most often, melanomas arise in the skin (cutaneous melanomas) and include the following histologic subtypes: superficial spreading melanoma, nodular melanoma, acral lentiginous melanoma, and lentigo maligna melanoma. Cutaneous melanomas may arise from acquired or congenital melanocytic or dysplastic nevi. Melanomas may also arise in other anatomic sites including the gastrointestinal system, eye, urinary tract, and reproductive system. Melanomas frequently metastasize to lymph nodes, liver, lungs, and brain. [NCIT: C3224]

OBA_2045215.title: menstrual cycle attribute
OBA_2045215.description: The quality of a menstrual cycle. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

MONDO:0005084.title: mental or behavioural disorder
MONDO:0005084.description: A disease that has its basis in the disruption of mental process. [MONDO: patterns/basis_in_disruption_of_process]

excluded_pgs.method: integrative PRS of 24 component PRSs derived from LDPRED-2, C+T, PRS-CS

EFO_0010906.title: merkel cell virus seropositivity
EFO_0010906.description: The result of a measurement of circulating merkel cell virus-specific antibodies.

MONDO:0005066.title: metabolic disease
MONDO:0005066.description: A congenital disorder (due to inherited enzyme abnormality) or acquired (due to failure of a metabolically important organ) disorder resulting from an abnormal metabolic process. [NCIT: C3235]

MONDO:0000226.title: mineral metabolism disease
MONDO:0000226.description: Abnormal levels of minerals in the blood.

HP_0012594.title: Moderate albuminuria
HP_0012594.description: The presence of moderately increased concentrations of albumin in the urine, defined as and albumin-creatinine ratio (ACR) of 30 to 299 mg/gm (3.4 to 34 mg/mmol). [HPO_CONTRIBUTOR: Eurenomics_fschaefer] [PMID: 30571025]

EFO_0005091.title: monocyte count
EFO_0005091.description: quantification of monocytes in the blood

EFO_0007989.title: monocyte percentage of leukocytes
EFO_0007989.description: A calculated measurement in which the number of monocytes in a specified sample of blood is divided by the total number of white blood cells in the sample, and the result presented as a ratio, fraction, quotient or percentage.

MONDO:0001397.title: mononeuropathy
MONDO:0001397.description: Disease or trauma involving a single peripheral nerve in isolation, or out of proportion to evidence of diffuse peripheral nerve dysfunction. Mononeuropathy multiplex refers to a condition characterized by multiple isolated nerve injuries. Mononeuropathies may result from a wide variety of causes, including ischemia; traumatic injury; compression; connective tissue diseases; cumulative trauma disorders; and other conditions. [MESH: D020422]

EFO_0007783.title: mosaic loss of chromosome Y measurement

MONDO:0000334.title: multinodular goiter
MONDO:0000334.description: Nodular goiter characterized by more than one discrete tissue mass. [NCIT: C131438]

MONDO:0009693.title: multiple myeloma
MONDO:0009693.description: A bone marrow-based plasma cell neoplasm characterized by a serum monoclonal protein and skeletal destruction with osteolytic lesions, pathological fractures, bone pain, hypercalcemia, and anemia. Clinical variants include non-secretory myeloma, smoldering myeloma, indolent myeloma, and plasma cell leukemia. (WHO, 2001) [NCIT: C3242]

MONDO:0005068.title: myocardial infarction
MONDO:0005068.description: Gross necrosis of the myocardium, as a result of interruption of the blood supply to the area, as in coronary thrombosis. [NCIT: C27996]

MONDO:0024644.title: Myocardial Ischemia
MONDO:0024644.description: A disorder of cardiac function caused by insufficient blood flow to the muscle tissue of the heart. The decreased blood flow may be due to narrowing of the coronary arteries (coronary artery disease), to obstruction by a thrombus (coronary thrombosis), or less commonly, to diffuse narrowing of arterioles and other small vessels within the heart. Severe interruption of the blood supply to the myocardial tissue may result in necrosis of cardiac muscle (myocardial infarction). [MESH: D017202]

HP_0000545.title: Myopia
HP_0000545.description: An abnormality of refraction characterized by the ability to see objects nearby clearly, while objects in the distance appear blurry.

MONDO:0009718.title: myxedema
MONDO:0009718.description: A condition characterized by severe hypothyroidism that is caused by autoimmune thyroid gland disorders, surgical reduction of thyroid tissue, radiation exposure, and viral infections. Signs and symptoms include generalized fatigue, lethargy, increased body weight, pale, edematous and thickened skin, low blood pressure, constipation and cold intolerance. [NCIT: C34834]

EFO_0009818.title: nap during day, self-reported
EFO_0009818.description: Having a nap during the day, as reported by an individual.

MONDO:0021107.title: narcolepsy
MONDO:0021107.description: A sleep disorder characterized by a tendency for excessive sleepiness during the day which occurs even after adequate sleep in the nighttime. The persons who suffer from this condition experience fatigue and may fall asleep at inappropriate times during the day. [NCIT: C84489]

MONDO:0016158.title: narcolepsy-cataplexy syndrome
MONDO:0016158.description: A type of narcolepsy characterized by excessive day-time sleepiness associated with uncontrollable sleep urges and cataplexy (loss of muscle tone often triggered by pleasant emotions). [Orphanet: 2073]

MONDO:0006314.title: Nasal Cavity Polyp
MONDO:0006314.description: A soft and painless polypoid mass that arises from the mucosa in the nasal cavity. It is usually the result of an inflammatory process. It may recur following surgical resection. [NCIT: C3256]

HP_0100582.title: Nasal polyposis
HP_0100582.description: Polypoidal masses arising mainly from the mucous membranes of the nose and paranasal sinuses. They are freely movable and nontender overgrowths of the mucosa that frequently accompany allergic rhinitis. [PMID: 18728843]

MONDO:0005375.title: nasopharyngeal neoplasm
MONDO:0005375.description: A benign or malignant neoplasm affecting the nasopharynx. Representative examples of benign neoplasms include angiofibroma and squamous papilloma. Representative examples of malignant neoplasms include keratinizing squamous cell carcinoma and nonkeratinizing carcinoma. [NCIT: C3257]

MONDO:0008171.title: nephrolithiasis
MONDO:0008171.description: The presence of a calculus in the pelvis of the kidney; this is most often composed of mineral salts and proteins. [NCIT: P378]

MONDO:0005377.title: nephrotic syndrome
MONDO:0005377.description: A collection of symptoms that include severe edema, proteinuria, and hypoalbuminemia; it is indicative of renal dysfunction. [NCIT: C34845]

MONDO:0005072.title: neuroblastoma
MONDO:0005072.description: Neuroblastoma (NB) is the most common solid, extracranial childhood tumor. It is an aggressive pediatric cancer that originates from neural crest tissues of the sympathetic nervous system. [PMID: 32903387]

EFO_0004346.title: neuroimaging measurement
EFO_0004346.description: Non-invasive methods of visualizing and measuring the CENTRAL NERVOUS SYSTEM, especially the brain, by various imaging modalities e.g. CT scan | is about MESH:D059906

EFO_0007660.title: neuroticism measurement
EFO_0007660.description: Quantification of some aspect of neuroticism or neurotic behaviour, such as severity or frequency of neurotic behaviour. This is usually assessed via a standardised questionnaire.

EFO_0004833.title: neutrophil count
EFO_0004833.description: The number of granulocytes (polymorphonuclear leukocytes) in a specified volume of blood, usually 1 cubic millimeter.

EFO_0007990.title: neutrophil percentage of leukocytes
EFO_0007990.description: A calculated measurement in which the number of neutrophil granulocytes is divided by the number of all white blood cells in a specified sample of peripheral venous blood, and the result presented as a ratio, fraction, quotient or percentage. A neutrophil is, or is in the process of becoming, a granular leucocyte having a nucleus with three to five lobes connected by threads of chromatin, cytoplasm containing very fine granules, and which is readily stainable with neutral dyes.

MONDO:0008575.title: nicotine dependence
MONDO:0008575.description: Physical and psychological dependence on nicotine. [NCIT: C54203]

EFO_0007794.title: nicotine metabolite ratio
EFO_0007794.description: quantification of the ratio of 3’-trans-hydroxycotinine to cotinine in a sample, typically in a urine or blood sample

MONDO:0013209.title: non-alcoholic fatty liver disease
MONDO:0013209.description: Metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease (MASLD, formerly known as nonalcoholic fatty liver disease or NAFLD) is a type of liver disease that is not caused by alcohol. It typically does not cause symptoms in the early stages, but it can cause health problems due to fat accumulation, inflammation, and scarring in the liver.

EFO_0008421.title: non-alcoholic fatty liver disease severity measurement
EFO_0008421.description: Quantification of the severity of non-alcoholic fatty liver disease, typically by a histological grading.

EFO_0005689.title: non-high density lipoprotein cholesterol measurement
EFO_0005689.description: The quantification of NHDL cholesterol in blood, calculated by subtracting the amount of cholesterol in the HDL fraction from total cholesterol and used as an indicator for cardiovascular disease risk

MONDO:0018908.title: non-Hodgkins lymphoma
MONDO:0018908.description: Distinct from Hodgkin lymphoma both morphologically and biologically, non-Hodgkin lymphoma (NHL) is characterized by the absence of Reed-Sternberg cells, can occur at any age, and usually presents as a localized or generalized lymphadenopathy associated with fever and weight loss. The clinical course varies according to the morphologic type. NHL is clinically classified as indolent, aggressive, or having a variable clinical course. NHL can be of B-or T-/NK-cell lineage. [NCIT: C3211]

EFO_0009260.title: non-melanoma skin carcinoma
EFO_0009260.description: Any skin carcinoma that is not melanoma, with the most common types being basal cell carcinoma and squamous cell carcinoma

EFO_0008586.title: Non-ST Elevation Myocardial Infarction
EFO_0008586.description: An electrocardiographic finding of a myocardial infarction that does not produce elevations in the ST segment of the EKG. [ NCI ]

MONDO:0001658.title: nontoxic goiter
MONDO:0001658.description: Sporadic enlargement of the thyroid gland that is not associated with changes in thyroid function or malignancy. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_0007012.title: NSAID use measurement
EFO_0007012.description: quantification of some aspect of NSAID use

OBA_2050020.title: nucleus accumbens volume
OBA_2050020.description: The volume of a nucleus accumbens. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

EFO_0009800.title: number of cancers, self-reported
EFO_0009800.description: Number of cancer occurrences in an individual, as reported by the individual.

EFO_0009102.title: number of children ever born measurement
EFO_0009102.description: A measurement of the number of children born

EFO_0009801.title: number of non-cancer illnesses, self-reported
EFO_0009801.description: Number of non-cancer illness occurrences in an individual, as reported by the individual.

EFO_0009803.title: number of treatments or medications taken, self-reported
EFO_0009803.description: Number of treatments undergone by an individual (including medications taken), as reported by the individual.

MONDO:0011122.title: obesity
MONDO:0011122.description: A disorder involving an excessive amount of body fat.

MONDO:0007147.title: obstructive sleep apnea
MONDO:0007147.description: Cessation of air flow during sleep due to upper airway obstruction. [NCIT: P378]

MONDO:0002236.title: ocular cancer
MONDO:0002236.description: A benign or malignant neoplasm affecting the structures of the eye. [NCIT: P378]

MONDO:0019433.title: oligoarticular juvenile idiopathic arthritis
MONDO:0019433.description: Oligoarticular juvenile arthritis is the most common form of juvenile idiopathic arthritis (JIA), representing nearly 50% of cases. [Orphanet: 85410]

MONDO:0005338.title: open-angle glaucoma
MONDO:0005338.description: Chronic outflow obstruction of the eye's drainage canals that can lead to increased internal eye pressure and optic nerve damage. [NCIT: P378]

MONDO:0005515.title: oral cavity cancer
MONDO:0005515.description: A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm involving the oral cavity. The majority are squamous cell carcinomas. [NCIT: C9314]

HP_0000155.title: Oral ulcer
HP_0000155.description: Erosion of the mucous mebrane of the mouth with local excavation of the surface, resulting from the sloughing of inflammatory necrotic tissue.

MONDO:0005178.title: osteoarthritis
MONDO:0005178.description: A noninflammatory degenerative joint disease occurring chiefly in older persons, characterized by degeneration of the articular cartilage, hypertrophy of bone at the margins and changes in the synovial membrane. It is accompanied by pain and stiffness, particularly after prolonged activity. [NCIT: C3293]

MONDO:0006629.title: osteoarthritis, hip
MONDO:0006629.description: Noninflammatory degenerative disease of the hip joint which usually appears in late middle or old age. It is characterized by growth or maturational disturbances in the femoral neck and head, as well as acetabular dysplasia. A dominant symptom is pain on weight-bearing or motion. [MESH: D015207]

MONDO:0005416.title: osteoarthritis, knee
MONDO:0005416.description: Noninflammatory degenerative disease of the knee joint consisting of three large categories: conditions that block normal synchronous movement, conditions that produce abnormal pathways of motion, and conditions that cause stress concentration resulting in changes to articular cartilage. (Crenshaw, Campbell's Operative Orthopaedics, 8th ed, p2019) [MESH: D020370]

MONDO:0005298.title: osteoporosis
MONDO:0005298.description: A condition of reduced bone mass, with decreased cortical thickness and a decrease in the number and size of the trabeculae of cancellous bone (but normal chemical composition), resulting in increased fracture incidence. Osteoporosis is classified as primary (Type 1, postmenopausal osteoporosis; Type 2, age-associated osteoporosis; and idiopathic, which can affect juveniles, premenopausal women, and middle-aged men) and secondary osteoporosis (which results from an identifiable cause of bone mass loss). [NCIT: C3298]

MONDO:0005349.title: otosclerosis
MONDO:0005349.description: Formation of spongy bone in the labyrinth capsule which can progress toward the stapes (stapedial fixation) or anteriorly toward the cochlea leading to conductive, sensorineural, or mixed hearing loss. Several genes are associated with familial otosclerosis with varied clinical signs. [MESH: D010040]

MONDO:0005140.title: ovarian carcinoma
MONDO:0005140.description: A malignant neoplasm originating from the surface ovarian epithelium. It accounts for the greatest number of deaths from malignancies of the female genital tract and is the fifth leading cause of cancer fatalities in women. It is predominantly a disease of older white women of northern European extraction, but it is seen in all ages and ethnic groups. Adenocarcinomas constitute the vast majority of ovarian carcinomas. The pattern of metastatic spread in ovarian carcinoma is similar regardless of the microscopic type. The most common sites of involvement are the contralateral ovary, peritoneal cavity, para-aortic and pelvic lymph nodes, and liver. Lung and pleura are the most common sites of extra-abdominal spread. The primary form of therapy is surgical. The overall prognosis of ovarian carcinoma remains poor, a direct result of its rapid growth rate and the lack of early symptoms. --2002 [NCIT: C4908]

MONDO:0021068.title: ovarian neoplasm
MONDO:0021068.description: A benign, borderline, or malignant neoplasm involving the ovary. [NCIT: C4984]

EFO_0005935.title: overweight body mass index status
EFO_0005935.description: quantification of weight/height relationships above a certain threshold that classes an individual as overweight. This threshold may vary based on an individual's ethnicity but is commonly considered to correspond in European populations to a 25<BMI<30

OBA_2050027.title: pallidum volume
OBA_2050027.description: The volume of a pallidum. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

MONDO:0002356.title: pancreas disease
MONDO:0002356.description: A non-neoplastic or neoplastic disorder that affects the pancreas. Representative examples of non-neoplastic disorders include pancreatitis and pancreatic insufficiency. Representative examples of neoplastic disorders include cystadenomas, carcinomas, lymphomas, and neuroendocrine neoplasms. [NCIT: C26842]

MONDO:0005192.title: pancreatic carcinoma
MONDO:0005192.description: A carcinoma that arises from epithelial cells of the exocrine pancreas

MONDO:0021040.title: pancreatic neoplasm
MONDO:0021040.description: A benign or malignant neoplasm involving the pancreas. [NCIT: C3305]

OBA_2050005.title: parasubiculum volume
OBA_2050005.description: The volume of a parasubiculum. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

EFO_0009718.title: peak expiratory flow
EFO_0009718.description: The maximum rate of gas flow, beginning at the point of peak inspiratory capacity, that can be sustained during forced exhalation for a defined period of time. | The maximum rate of gas flow, beginning at the point of peak inspiratory capacity, that can be sustained during forced exhalation for a defined period of time. This test is most often used in combination with other studies of lung function to diagnose and monitor obstructive and restrictive lung disease(s). [ NCI ]

MONDO:0000082.title: pelvic organ prolapse
MONDO:0000082.description: Abnormal descent of a pelvic organ resulting in the protrusion of the organ beyond its normal anatomical confines. Symptoms often include vaginal discomfort, dyspareunia; urinary stress incontinence; and fecal incontinence. [MESH: D056887]

MONDO:0005386.title: peripheral arterial disease
MONDO:0005386.description: A disorder of the arteries supplying the upper and lower extremity and the visceral organs. This includes the mesenteric arteries, the renal arteries and the aorta and excludes cerebrovascular arterial disease. Patients experience cramping and pain usually in the calves and thighs while walking. The symptoms subside with rest. [NCIT: C84496]

MONDO:0003620.title: peripheral nervous system disease
MONDO:0003620.description: A disease involving the peripheral nervous system.

MONDO:0005294.title: peripheral vascular disease
MONDO:0005294.description: Any disorder affecting blood flow through the veins or arteries outside of the heart. [NCIT: C35136]

MONDO:0005517.title: pharynx cancer
MONDO:0005517.description: A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm that affects the pharynx. [NCIT: C7545]

EFO_0010968.title: phosphate measurement
EFO_0010968.description: Quantification of phosphate levels in a sample.

EFO_0008002.title: physical activity measurement
EFO_0008002.description: quantification of some aspect of physical activity such as frequency or intensity

EFO_0007984.title: platelet component distribution width
EFO_0007984.description: Measure of variation in platelet size. | The determination of the amount of platelet shape changes present in a sample. [ NCI ]

EFO_0004309.title: platelet count
EFO_0004309.description: The number of PLATELETS per unit volume in a sample of venous BLOOD.

EFO_0007985.title: platelet crit
EFO_0007985.description: The proportion of blood volume that is occupied by platelets, expressed as a percentage.

OBA_0003277.title: platelet volume
OBA_0003277.description: The volume of a platelet. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

MONDO:0019432.title: polyarticular juvenile idiopathic arthritis, rheumatoid factor negative
MONDO:0019432.description: Rheumatoid factor-negative polyarthritis is a term used to describe a group of poorly defined heterogenous conditions that incorporates forms of rheumatoid factor-negative polyarthritis and forms of oligoarticular arthritis that become extensive in less than 6 months after onset. [Orphanet: 85408]

MONDO:0009891.title: polycythemia vera
MONDO:0009891.description: Polycythemia vera (PV) is an acquired myeloproliferative disorder characterized by an elevated absolute red blood cell mass caused by uncontrolled red blood cell production, frequently associated with uncontrolled white blood cell and platelet production. [Orphanet: 729]

MONDO:0019735.title: polymyalgia rheumatica
MONDO:0019735.description: A syndrome characterized by pain, stiffness, and tenderness of the proximal muscle groups including the shoulder, pelvic girdle and the neck. There is no muscle atrophy and muscle biopsies do not reveal pathologic changes. Additional signs and symptoms include low grade fever, fatigue and depression. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_0010907.title: polyomavirus 2 seropositivity
EFO_0010907.description: The result of a measurement of circulating polyomavirus 2-specific antibodies.

MONDO:0037821.title: porphyrin metabolism disease
MONDO:0037821.description: A disease that has its basis in the disruption of porphyrin-containing compound metabolic process. [MONDO: patterns/basis_in_disruption_of_process]

EFO_0600085.title: PP interval
EFO_0600085.description: An interval measured from the onset of the P wave in one PQRST wave complex to the onset of the P wave in the following PQRST wave complex.

MONDO:0005081.title: preeclampsia
MONDO:0005081.description: Preeclampsia is a hypertensive disorder of pregnancy that is characterized by new-onset hypertension with proteinuria presenting after 20 weeks of gestation, and depending on mild or severe forms may initially present with severe headache, visual disturbances, and hyperreflexia. [Orphanet: 275555]

OBA_2050049.title: presubiculum volume
OBA_2050049.description: The volume of a presubiculum. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

EFO_0004462.title: PR interval
EFO_0004462.description: A PR interval is an  electrocardiography measurement which measures from the beginning of the P wave to the beginning of the QRS complex in the heart's electrical cycle

EFO_0010814.title: proinsulin measurement
EFO_0010814.description: Measurement of proinsulin, an insulin precursor that is processed into mature insulin and C-peptide.

MONDO:0005082.title: prostate adenocarcinoma
MONDO:0005082.description: A carcinoma that arises from glandular epithelial cells of the prostate gland [MONDO: DesignPattern]

MONDO:0005159.title: prostate carcinoma
MONDO:0005159.description: A carcinoma that arises from epithelial cells of the prostate gland.

Independent genome-wide significant variants included, identified from fine-mapping GWAS results.

Independent genome-wide significant variants included, identified from fine-mapping GWAS results. Variants were lifted-over from GRCh37 to GRCh38, proxy variants in TOPMED imputation panel were selected if no exact variant was available in TOPMed.

MONDO:0003105.title: prostate disease
MONDO:0003105.description: A disease involving the prostate gland.

OBA_2050200.title: prostate specific antigen amount
OBA_2050200.description: The amount of a prostate-specific antigen when measured in anatomical entity. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute_location]

MONDO:0026045.title: prurigo nodularis
MONDO:0026045.description: Prurigonodularis (PN) is a skin disease in which hard crusty lumps are formed on the skin that itches intensely. Repetitive rubbing, scratching, and touching results in more lesions in the skin. The itching is so intense that people scratch themselves to the point of bleeding or pain. The lumps formed in the skin are hard, and have about a half inch across, with a dry and rough top that is often scratched open. They tend to be located in the areas most easily reached and are worse on the outer sides: arms, shoulders and legs. The trunk, face and even palms can also be affected. The exact cause is unknown but some factors triggering PN include liver and kidneys problems, nervous and mental conditions and other skin diseases. Prurigo nodulares, in some cases, can be seen in other diseases such as lymphoma, chronic autoimmune cholestatic hepatitis, HIV infection, severe anemia,or a chronic kidney disease-related itching known as uremic pruritus. Treatment is very difficult, and may include corticoids, antihistaminic and other medication such as thalidomide and similar (Lenalidomide). In some cases, cryotherapy, photochemotherapy and habit reversal therapy for the itch-scratch cycle has improved the symptoms. PN can last for many years, and the itching is so intense that may affect all the everyday activities. [GARD: 0007480]

MONDO:0005083.title: psoriasis
MONDO:0005083.description: An autoimmune condition characterized by red, well-delineated plaques with silvery scales that are usually on the extensor surfaces and scalp. They can occasionally present with these manifestations: pustules; erythema and scaling in intertriginous areas, and erythroderma, that are often distributed on extensor surfaces and scalp. [NCIT: P378]

MONDO:0011849.title: psoriatic arthritis
MONDO:0011849.description: Joint inflammation associated with psoriasis. [NCIT: P378]

MONDO:0005279.title: pulmonary embolism
MONDO:0005279.description: The obstruction of the pulmonary artery or one of its branches by an embolus, sometimes associated with infarction of the lung. [NCIT: P378]

MONDO:0002771.title: pulmonary fibrosis
MONDO:0002771.description: Chronic progressive interstitial lung disorder characterized by the replacement of the lung tissue by connective tissue, leading to progressive dyspnea, respiratory failure, or right heart failure. Causes include chronic inflammatory processes, exposure to environmental irritants, radiation therapy, autoimmune disorders, certain drugs, or it may be idiopathic (no identifiable cause). [NCIT: P378]

EFO_0005763.title: pulse pressure measurement
EFO_0005763.description: quantification of the difference between systolic blood pressure and diastolic blood pressure. Higher PP is associated with left ventricle hypertrophy and the increased intimal thickness of the carotid artery, which represent early target organ damage in cardiovascular diseases

OBA_2050016.title: putamen volume
OBA_2050016.description: The volume of a putamen. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

EFO_0005094.title: P wave duration
EFO_0005094.description: measurement of the time from start to finish of the P wave, in the electrocardiograph illustrating the initial deflection of the cardiac cycle, representing the excitation of the atria.

EFO_0004682.title: QT interval
EFO_0004682.description: The QT interval is a measure of the time between the start of the Q wave and the end of the T wave in the heart's electrical cycle

MONDO:0005325.title: radius fracture
MONDO:0005325.description: Traumatic or pathological injury to the radius bone in which the continuity of the bone is broken. [EFO: 0003957]

MONDO:0017376.title: reactive arthritis
MONDO:0017376.description: Reactive arthritis (ReA) is an autoimmune disorder belonging to the group of seronegative spondyloarthropathies and is characterized by the classic triad of arthritis, urethritis and conjunctivitis. [Orphanet: 29207]

MONDO:0002423.title: rectosigmoid junction neoplasm
MONDO:0002423.description: A benign or malignant neoplasm that affects the rectosigmoid region. Representative examples of benign neoplasms include lipoma and leiomyoma. Representative examples of malignant neoplasms include carcinoma, lymphoma, and sarcoma. Rectosigmoid adenomas always exhibit epithelial dysplasia and are considered premalignant neoplasms. [NCIT: C4877]

MONDO:0006519.title: rectum cancer
MONDO:0006519.description: A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm that affects the rectum. Representative examples include carcinoma, lymphoma, and sarcoma. [NCIT: C7418]

EFO_0010246.title: recurrent
EFO_0010246.description: Episodes of disease that occur in individuals who have already had the disease.

EFO_0009188.title: Red cell distribution width
EFO_0009188.description: Red blood cell distribution width (RDW or RDW-CV or RCDW and RDW-SD) is a measure of the range of variation of red blood cell (RBC) volume that is reported as part of a standard complete blood count. Usually red blood cells are a standard size of about 6-8 μm in diameter. Certain disorders, however, cause a significant variation in cell size. Higher RDW values indicate greater variation in size. Normal reference range of RDW-CV in human red blood cells is 11.5-14.5%.[1] If anemia is observed, RDW test results are often used together with mean corpuscular volume (MCV) results to determine the possible causes of the anemia. It is mainly used to differentiate an anemia of mixed causes from an anemia of a single cause.

EFO_0007814.title: refractive error measurement
EFO_0007814.description: quantification of some aspect of refractive errors, which occur when the shape of the eye prevents light from focusing directly on the retina. The length of the eyeball (longer or shorter), changes in the shape of the cornea, or aging of the lens can cause refractive errors. Quantification of an individual's refractive error allows the establishemnt of a number of corrective parameters such as spherical equivalent, lens decentration and transposition

MONDO:0005937.title: REM sleep behavior disorder
MONDO:0005937.description: A disorder characterized by episodes of vigorous and often violent motor activity during rem sleep (sleep, rem). The affected individual may inflict self injury or harm others, and is difficult to awaken from this condition. Episodes are usually followed by a vivid recollection of a dream that is consistent with the aggressive behavior. This condition primarily affects adult males. (From Adams et al., Principles of Neurology, 6th ed, p393) [MESH: D020187]

MONDO:0005206.title: renal carcinoma
MONDO:0005206.description: A carcinoma arising from the epithelium of the renal parenchyma or the renal pelvis. The majority are renal cell carcinomas. Kidney carcinomas usually affect middle aged and elderly adults. Hematuria, abdominal pain, and a palpable mass are common symptoms. [NCIT: C9384]

MONDO:0005086.title: renal cell carcinoma
MONDO:0005086.description: A carcinoma that arises from glandular epithelial cells of the kidney

OBA_2050117.title: reproductive behavior trait
OBA_2050117.description: The quality of a reproductive behavior. [obo: oba/patterns/entity_attribute_part_of]

MONDO:0000376.title: respiratory system cancer
MONDO:0000376.description: A malignant neoplasm involving the respiratory system

MONDO:0005087.title: respiratory system disease
MONDO:0005087.description: A non-neoplastic or neoplastic disorder that affects the respiratory system. Representative examples include pneumonia, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, pulmonary failure, lung adenoma, lung carcinoma, and tracheal carcinoma. [NCIT: C26871]

excluded_pgs.method: Clumped lung cancer loci (35 SNP score) combined with LASSO penalized regression (93 SNP score)

MONDO:0024355.title: respiratory tract infectious disorder
MONDO:0024355.description: Invasion of the host RESPIRATORY SYSTEM by microorganisms, usually leading to pathological processes or diseases. [MESH: D012141]

OBA_0002272.title: reticulocyte amount
OBA_0002272.description: The amount of a reticulocyte. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

EFO_0007986.title: reticulocyte count
EFO_0007986.description: The number of reticulocytes per unit volume of blood. Reticulocytes are immature red blood cells and typically compose aoubt 1% of red blood cells in the human body.

EFO_0010698.title: retinal break
EFO_0010698.description: Retinal breaks are full-thickness discontinuities in the retina. They can be classified into tears, such as horseshoe tears and giant retinal tears, or holes, such as operculated holes and atrophic holes.

MONDO:0008375.title: retinal detachment
MONDO:0008375.description: An eye emergency condition which may lead to blindness if left untreated. It is characterized by the separation of the inner retina layers from the underlying pigment epithelium. Causes include trauma, advanced diabetes mellitus, high myopia, and choroid tumors. Symptoms include sudden appearance of floaters, sudden light flushes, and blurred vision. [NCIT: P378]

MONDO:0005283.title: retinopathy
MONDO:0005283.description: Any disease or disorder of the retina. [NCIT: C62601]

MONDO:0008383.title: rheumatoid arthritis
MONDO:0008383.description: A chronic, systemic autoimmune disorder characterized by inflammation in the synovial membranes and articular surfaces. It manifests primarily as a symmetric, erosive polyarthritis that spares the axial skeleton and is typically associated with the presence in the serum of rheumatoid factor. [NCIT: C2884]

EFO_0005767.title: rheumatoid factor measurement
EFO_0005767.description: quantification of rheumatoid factors, antibodies in the serum of individuals with rheumatoid arthritis that react with antigenic determinants or immunoglobulins that enhance agglutination of suspended particles coated with pooled human gamma -globulin. Rheumatoid factors also occur in other autoimmune and certain infectious diseases.

MONDO:0006953.title: Rh isoimmunization
MONDO:0006953.description: The mother develops antibodies against red blood cell Rhesus antigens. This may lead to potential fetal adverse outcomes such as anemia. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_0008579.title: risk-taking behaviour
EFO_0008579.description: The tendency to take risks. Risk-taking behaviour is a characteristic of several psychiatric disorders such as ADHD and bipolar disorder, as well as problem behaviours such as smoking and drug abuse.

EFO_0004831.title: RR interval
EFO_0004831.description: A RR interval is an  electrocardiography measurement  - R wave to R wave interval (RR interval) and is the inverse of the heart rate.

MONDO:0019338.title: sarcoidosis
MONDO:0019338.description: Sarcoidosis is a multisystemic disorder of unknown cause characterized by the formation of immune granulomas in involved organs. [Orphanet: 797]

MONDO:0005324.title: seasonal allergic rhinitis
MONDO:0005324.description: Allergic rhinitis caused by outdoor allergens. [NCIT: C92188]

MONDO:0008420.title: seborrheic keratosis
MONDO:0008420.description: A common benign skin neoplasm usually affecting older individuals. The lesions usually are multiple and arise in the face, chest, and shoulders. They appear as black or brown, slightly elevated skin lesions. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_0009820.title: seeing a general practitioner for nerves, anxiety, tension or depression, self-reported
EFO_0009820.description: Seeing a general practitioner for nerves, anxiety, tension or depression, as reported by an individual.

EFO_0009821.title: seeing a psychiatrist for nerves, anxiety, tension or depression, self-reported
EFO_0009821.description: Seeing a psychiatrist for nerves, anxiety, tension or depression, as reported by an individual.

HP_0100716.title: Self-injurious behavior
HP_0100716.description: Self-aggression. | All of these actions can be impulsive, compulsive, or planned.

EFO_0009799.title: self-reported trait
EFO_0009799.description: Characteristics of an individual that are reported by the individual, usually to medical staff, including via questionnaires, rather than observed or measured directly by medical staff.

OBA_2050062.title: serum alanine aminotransferase amount
OBA_2050062.description: The amount of a alanine aminotransferase when measured in blood serum. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute_location]

OBA_2050068.title: serum albumin amount
OBA_2050068.description: The amount of a albumin when measured in blood serum. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute_location]

OBA_2050096.description: The amount of a creatinine when measured in blood serum. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute_location]

EFO_0004532.title: serum gamma-glutamyl transferase measurement
EFO_0004532.description: Serum gamma-glutamyl transferase level measurement is the quantification of gamma-glutamyl transferase in blood. Gamma-glutamyl transferase is used as a marker for liver/bile duct problems and alcohol abuse.

OBA_2050084.title: serum IgE amount
OBA_2050084.description: The amount of a IgE immunoglobulin complex, circulating when measured in blood serum. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute_location]

OBA_2050065.title: serum urea amount
OBA_2050065.description: The amount of a urea when measured in blood serum. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute_location]

EFO_0004696.title: sex hormone-binding globulin measurement
EFO_0004696.description: Is a quantification of sex hormone binding globulin. In men the test is used in when investigating testosterone deficiency.  The SHBG glycoprotein binds to circulating estradiol and testosterone, low levels of SHBG may be implicated in breast cancer etiology.

EFO_0011011.title: sitting height measurement
EFO_0011011.description: Quantification of some aspect of height, when assuming a sitting position.

MONDO:0010030.title: Sjogren syndrome
MONDO:0010030.description: An autoimmune disorder in which immune cells attack and destroy the glands that produce tears and saliva. Sjögren syndrome is also associated with rheumatic disorders such as rheumatoid arthritis or systemic lupus erythematosus. The hallmark symptoms of Sjögren syndrome are dry mouth and dry eyes. In addition, Sjogren syndrome may cause skin, nose, and vaginal dryness. It also may affect other organs of the body including the kidneys, blood vessels, lungs, liver, pancreas, and brain

EFO_0005422.title: skin aging
EFO_0005422.description: The gradual irreversible changes in structure of skin that occur as a result of the passage of time.. In humans, skin aging can be precipitated as a result of weather and sun exposure, and expresses through the appearance of wrinkles and localised changes in skin pigmentation

MONDO:0002898.title: skin cancer
MONDO:0002898.description: A malignant neoplasm involving the zone of skin

MONDO:0002656.title: skin carcinoma
MONDO:0002656.description: A carcinoma that arises from epithelial cells of the zone of skin

MONDO:0004641.title: skin carcinoma in situ
MONDO:0004641.description: A in situ carcinoma that involves the zone of skin. [MONDO: patterns/location]

MONDO:0005093.title: skin disease
MONDO:0005093.description: Any deviation from the normal structure or function of the skin or subcutaneous tissue that is manifested by a characteristic set of symptoms and signs. [NCIT: C3371]

MONDO:0006611.title: skin sarcoidosis
MONDO:0006611.description: Formation of non-necrotizing granulomas in the skin. It may be a manifestation of systemic sarcoidosis or may also arise in isolation. [NCIT: P378]

MONDO:0005296.title: sleep apnea
MONDO:0005296.description: A disorder characterized by multiple cessations of respirations during sleep that induce partial arousals and interfere with the maintenance of sleep. [MESH: D012891]

MONDO:0100081.title: Sleep Disorder
MONDO:0100081.description: A change from the patient's baseline sleeping pattern, in the hours slept and/or an alteration/dysfunction in the stages of sleep. [NCIT: C3376]

OBA_2040171.title: sleep duration trait
OBA_2040171.description: The duration of a sleep. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

OBA_2050116.title: smoking behavior trait
OBA_2050116.description: The quality of a smoking behavior. [obo: oba/patterns/entity_attribute_part_of]

EFO_0004319.title: smoking cessation
EFO_0004319.description: Discontinuation of the habit of smoking, the inhaling and exhaling of tobacco smoke.

EFO_0005670.title: smoking initiation
EFO_0005670.description: initation of the habit of smoking, the inhaling and exhaling of tobacco smoke.

EFO_0006527.title: smoking status measurement
EFO_0006527.description: self-reported binary measure, used to denote whether an individual is currently or has ever been a smoker

EFO_0008341.title: snoring measurement
EFO_0008341.description: quantification of some aspect of snoring such as frequency or severity

EFO_0009592.title: social interaction measurement
EFO_0009592.description: Quantification, typically by a questionnaire, of some aspect of social interaction, such as engagement in social activities,  or degree of contact with friends and family.

EFO_0009282.title: sodium measurement
EFO_0009282.description: A quantitative measurement of the amount of sodium present in a sample.

EFO_0009470.title: soft tissue disease
EFO_0009470.description: A disease affecting tissues that connect, support, or surround other structures and organs of the body, not being hard tissue such as bone. Soft tissue includes tendons, ligaments, fascia, skin, fibrous tissues, fat, and synovial membranes (which are connective tissue), and muscles, nerves and blood vessels (which are not connective tissue).

MONDO:0005095.title: spondyloarthropathy
MONDO:0005095.description: A group of inflammatory rheumatic diseases associated with arthritis and enthesitis, and often involving the axial skeleton. The most common form of spondyloarthritis is ankylosing spondylitis. Other forms include axial spondyloarthritis, peripheral spondyloarthritis, reactive arthritis, psoriatic arthritis/spondylitis and enteropathic arthritis/spondylitis. [NCIT: C116778]

MONDO:0002253.title: spondylosis
MONDO:0002253.description: A degenerative spinal disease that can involve any part of the vertebra, the intervertebral disk, and the surrounding soft tissue. [MESH: D055009]

MONDO:0005096.title: squamous cell carcinoma
MONDO:0005096.description: A carcinoma arising from squamous epithelial cells. Morphologically, it is characterized by the proliferation of atypical, often pleomorphic squamous cells. Squamous cell carcinomas are graded by the degree of cellular differentiation as well, moderately, or poorly differentiated. Well differentiated carcinomas are usually associated with keratin production and the presence of intercellular bridges between adjacent cells. Representative examples are lung squamous cell carcinoma, skin squamous cell carcinoma, and cervical squamous cell carcinoma. [NCIT: C2929]

MONDO:0005097.title: squamous cell lung carcinoma
MONDO:0005097.description: A carcinoma arising from squamous bronchial epithelial cells. It may be keratinizing or non-keratinizing. Keratinizing squamous cell carcinoma is characterized by the presence of keratinization, pearl formation, and/or intercellular bridges. Non-keratinizing squamous cell carcinoma is characterized by the absence of keratinization, pearl formation, and intercellular bridges. Cigarette smoking and arsenic exposure are strongly associated with squamous cell lung carcinoma. [NCIT: C3493]

OBA_0002313.title: strand of hair color
OBA_0002313.description: The color of a strand of hair. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

MONDO:0005098.title: stroke
MONDO:0005098.description: A sudden loss of neurological function secondary to hemorrhage or ischemia in the brain parenchyma due to a vascular event. [NCIT: C3390]

excluded_pgs.method: Integrative PGS (also referred to as metaGRS) of 22 component PGSs
Integrative PGS (also referred to as metaGRS) of 22 component PGSs

OBA_2050024.title: subiculum volume
OBA_2050024.description: The volume of a subiculum. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

MONDO:0002494.title: substance-related disorder
MONDO:0002494.description: A category of psychiatric disorders which include disorders related to the taking of a drug of abuse (including alcohol, prescribed medications and recreational drugs). [NCIT: C92203]

EFO_0010158.title: sugar consumption measurement
EFO_0010158.description: Quantification of some aspect of sugar consumption such as frequency or quantity.

MONDO:0005326.title: sunburn
MONDO:0005326.description: An inflammatory reaction from ultraviolet radiation characterized by transient redness, tenderness and occasional blistering. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_0010729.title: sun exposure measurement
EFO_0010729.description: Quantification of some aspect of exposure to incident light from the sun.

EFO_0004279.title: suntan
EFO_0004279.description: An induced skin pigment ( MELANIN) darkening after exposure to SUNLIGHT or ULTRAVIOLET RAYS. The degree of tanning depends on the intensity and duration of UV exposure, and genetic factors.

EFO_0006516.title: superior frontal gyrus grey matter volume measurement
EFO_0006516.description: quantification of the volume of grey matter in the superior frontal gyrus, usuallly through an MRI scan

MONDO:0005100.title: systemic scleroderma
MONDO:0005100.description: A chronic disorder, possibly autoimmune, marked by excessive production of collagen which results in hardening and thickening of body tissues. The two types of systemic scleroderma, limited cutaneous and diffuse cutaneous are classified with focus on the extent of affected skin. A relationship exists between the extent of skin area affected and degree of internal organ/system involvement. Systemic scleroderma can manifest itself in pulmonary fibrosis, Raynaud's syndrome, digestive system telangiectasias, renal hypertension and/or pulmonary hypertension. [NCIT: C72070]

EFO_0006335.title: systolic blood pressure
EFO_0006335.description: The blood pressure during the contraction of the left ventricle of the heart.

EFO_0010091.title: tea consumption measurement
EFO_0010091.description: Quantification of some tea consumption or tea consumption-related behaviour, usually self-reported via a questionnaire.

MONDO:0005447.title: testicular carcinoma
MONDO:0005447.description: A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm that affects the testis. Representative examples include seminoma, embryonal carcinoma, sarcoma, leukemia, and lymphoma. [NCIT: C7251]

MONDO:0010108.title: Testicular Germ Cell Tumor
MONDO:0010108.description: A germ cell tumor arising from the testis. Representative examples include teratoma, seminoma, embryonal carcinoma, and yolk sac tumor. [NCIT: C8591]

EFO_0004281.title: testicular neoplasm
EFO_0004281.description: Tumors or cancer of the TESTIS. Germ cell tumors ( GERMINOMA) of the testis constitute 95% of all testicular neoplasms.

EFO_0006935.title: thalamus volume
EFO_0006935.description: quantification of the volume of the thalamus, a midline symmetrical structure of two halves, within the vertebrate brain, situated between the cerebral cortex and the midbrain

HP_0001873.title: Thrombocytopenia
HP_0001873.description: A reduction in the number of circulating thrombocytes.

HP_0004418.title: Thrombophlebitis
HP_0004418.description: Inflammation of a vein associated with venous thrombosis (blood clot formation within the vein).

MONDO:0000831.title: thrombotic disease
MONDO:0000831.description: The formation of a blood clot in the lumen of a vessel or heart chamber; causes include coagulation disorders and vascular endothelial injury. [NCIT: C26891]

MONDO:0015075.title: thyroid carcinoma
MONDO:0015075.description: A carcinoma arising from the thyroid gland. It is usually an adenocarcinoma and includes the following main subtypes: follicular, papillary, medullary, poorly differentiated, and anaplastic. [NCIT: C4815]

MONDO:0003240.title: thyroid disease
MONDO:0003240.description: A disease involving the thyroid gland.

EFO_0009933.title: Thyroid preparation use measurement
EFO_0009933.description: Quantification of some aspect of the use of thyroid preparations.

OBA_1000983.title: Thyroid stimulating hormone level
OBA_1000983.description: The amount of a thyroid stimulating hormone when measured in anatomical entity. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute_location]

MONDO:0010138.title: Thyrotoxicosis
MONDO:0010138.description: A hypermetabolic syndrome caused by the elevation of thyroid hormone levels in the serum. Signs and symptoms include tachycardia, palpitations, tremor, weight loss, warm weather intolerance, and moist skin. Causes include Graves disease, toxic nodular goiter, toxic thyroid nodule, and lymphocytic thyroiditis. [NCIT: P378]

OBA_1001010.title: thyroxine level
OBA_1001010.description: The amount of a thyroxine when measured in anatomical entity. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute_location]

EFO_0600081.title: time spent outdoors measurement
EFO_0600081.description: Quantification of time an individual spends outside.

HP_0000360.title: Tinnitus
HP_0000360.description: Tinnitus is an auditory perception that can be described as the experience of sound, in the ear or in the head, in the absence of external acoustic stimulation.

EFO_0007946.title: tiredness measurement
EFO_0007946.description: Quantification an individual's level of tiredness, generally via a standardised questionnaire or structured interview.

EFO_0009115.title: tobacco smoke exposure measurement
EFO_0009115.description: Quantification of some aspect of tobacco smoke exposure.

MONDO:0021240.title: tongue neoplasm
MONDO:0021240.description: A neoplasm (disease) that involves the tongue. [MONDO: patterns/location]

EFO_0004536.title: total blood protein measurement
EFO_0004536.description: A total blood protein measurement is a quantification of protein in a blood sample. It is used as a marker for disease and nutrition. It is performed in the liquid portion of blood and typically measures albumin and globulin.

EFO_0009806.title: total hip arthroplasty
EFO_0009806.description: A surgical procedure in which the hip joint is replaced by a prosthetic implant, that is, a hip prosthesis. A total hip replacement (total hip arthroplasty) consists of replacing both the acetabulum and the femoral head.

EFO_0020975.title: total knee arthroplasty
EFO_0020975.description: A surgical procedure in which the knee joint is replaced by a prosthetic implant.

MONDO:0001407.title: tracheal cancer
MONDO:0001407.description: A malignant neoplasm involving the trachea

EFO_0010703.title: trauma exposure measurement
EFO_0010703.description: Quantification of some aspect of trauma exposure.

MONDO:0005494.title: triple-negative breast cancer
MONDO:0005494.description: An invasive breast carcinoma which is negative for expression of estrogen receptor (ER), progesterone receptor (PR), and human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (HER2). [NCIT: P378]

MONDO:0005101.title: ulcerative colitis
MONDO:0005101.description: An inflammatory bowel disease involving the mucosal surface of the large intestine and rectum. It may present with an acute or slow onset and follows an intermittent or continuous course. Signs and symptoms include abdominal pain, diarrhea, fever, weight loss, and intestinal hemorrhage. [NCIT: P378]

HP_0001537.title: Umbilical hernia
HP_0001537.description: Protrusion of abdominal contents through a defect in the abdominal wall musculature around the umbilicus. Skin and subcutaneous tissue overlie the defect.

EFO_0009514.title: upper extremity fracture
EFO_0009514.description: A traumatic break in one or more of the bones in the arm, forearm, or hand. [ NCI ]

EFO_0004531.title: urate measurement
EFO_0004531.description: A urate measurement is the quantification of some urate in body fluid

EFO_0011005.title: urea measurement
EFO_0011005.description: Quantification of urea in a sample.

MONDO:0007009.title: ureterolithiasis
MONDO:0007009.description: The presence of a calculus in the ureter of the kidney; this is most often composed of mineral salts and proteins. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_0004761.title: uric acid measurement
EFO_0004761.description: Is a quantification of uric acid, typically in blood. Uric acid is associated with gout, insulin resistance and cardivascular disease.

EFO_0007778.title: urinary albumin to creatinine ratio
EFO_0007778.description: quantification of the ratio of albumin to creatinine in a urine sample

EFO_0005116.title: urinary metabolite measurement
EFO_0005116.description: quantification of some metabolite in urine

EFO_0010967.title: urinary microalbumin measurement
EFO_0010967.description: The quantification of microalbumin in urine.

MONDO:0002118.title: urinary system disease
MONDO:0002118.description: A disease involving the renal system.

OBA_2050095.title: urine potassium amount
OBA_2050095.description: The amount of a potassium atom when measured in urine. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute_location]

MONDO:0024647.title: urolithiasis
MONDO:0024647.description: Stone(s) within the urinary tract. [NCIT: C114688]

MONDO:0002715.title: uterine cancer
MONDO:0002715.description: Primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm involving the uterine corpus and/or the cervix. [NCIT: C3552]

MONDO:0005213.title: uterine carcinoma
MONDO:0005213.description: A carcinoma involving a uterus. [MONDO: patterns/carcinoma]

MONDO:0006003.title: uterine corpus cancer
MONDO:0006003.description: A malignant neoplasm involving the body of uterus.

MONDO:0007886.title: uterine fibroid
MONDO:0007886.description: A benign smooth muscle neoplasm arising from the body of the uterus. It is characterized by the presence of spindle cells with cigar-shaped nuclei, interlacing fascicles, and a whorled pattern. [NCIT: C3434]

EFO_0009340.title: Varicella zoster virus seropositivity
EFO_0009340.description: The result of a measurement of circulating Varicella zoster virus-specific antibodies

MONDO:0004648.title: vascular dementia
MONDO:0004648.description: A degenerative vascular disorder affecting the brain. It is caused by the blockage of the blood supply to the brain. It is manifested with decline of memory and cognitive functions. [NCIT: C34525]

MONDO:0006004.title: vasomotor rhinitis
MONDO:0006004.description: Inflammation in the nasal cavity mucosa that results from the abnormal regulation of the blood flow in the nose. It may be caused by temperature fluctuations, air pollution, strong odors, and tobacco smoke. It results in chronic nasal congestion, sneezing, and running nose. [NCIT: P378]

EFO_0005654.title: velocity of sound measurement
EFO_0005654.description: quantification of the speed at which sound travels through bone as an indicator of the physical and structural properties of the bone

MONDO:0005399.title: venous thromboembolism
MONDO:0005399.description: Occlusion of the lumen of a vein by a thrombus that has migrated from a distal site via the blood stream. [NCIT: C99537]

EFO_0007928.title: ventricular rate measurement
EFO_0007928.description: quantification in an electrocardiogram of the number of QRS complexes per minute

MONDO:0000812.title: vertebral column disorder
MONDO:0000812.description: A disease involving the vertebral column. [MONDO: patterns/location_top]

OBA_2045224.title: visceral adipose tissue quantity
OBA_2045224.description: The amount of a visceral fat. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

EFO_0004312.title: vital capacity
EFO_0004312.description: The volume of air that is exhaled by a maximal expiration following a maximal inspiration.

MONDO:0020696.title: vitamin B12 deficiency
MONDO:0020696.description: A disease characterized by low serum levels of vitamin B12, either inherited or acquired. [PMID: 16846473]

EFO_0004620.title: vitamin B12 measurement
EFO_0004620.description: is a quantification of Vitamin B12, vitamin B12 or vitamin B-12, is a water-solublevitamin with a key role in the normal functioning of the brain and nervous system, and for the formation of blood. It is one of the eight B vitamins. It is normally involved in themetabolism of every cell of the human body, especially affecting DNA synthesis and regulation, but also fatty acid synthesis and energy production. It is the largest and most structurally complicated vitamin and can be produced industrially only through bacterial fermentation-synthesis.

EFO_0009116.title: vitamin supplement exposure measurement
EFO_0009116.description: Quantification of some aspect of the consumption of dietry vitamin supplements.

MONDO:0008661.title: Vitiligo
MONDO:0008661.description: Generalized well circumscribed patches of leukoderma that are generally distributed over symmetric body locations and is due to autoimmune destruction of melanocytes. [NCIT: P378]

OBA_1001085.title: waist circumference
OBA_1001085.description: The circumference of a abdominal segment of trunk. [AUTO: patterns/patterns/entity_attribute]

EFO_0004343.title: waist-hip ratio
EFO_0004343.description: The waist circumference measurement divided by the hip circumference measurement. For both men and women, a waist-to-hip ratio (WHR) of 1.0 or higher is considered "at risk" for undesirable health consequences, such as heart disease and ailments associated with OVERWEIGHT. A healthy WHR is 0.90 or less for men, and 0.80 or less for women. (National Center for Chronic Disease Prevention and Health Promotion, 2004)

EFO_0007869.title: wellbeing measurement
EFO_0007869.description: quantification of some aspect of wellbeing such as life satisfaction or emotional wellbeing, usually self-reported and quantified through the use of a structured questionnaire

EFO_0005665.title: white matter hyperintensity measurement
EFO_0005665.description: quantification of white matter hyperintensity, usually measured by MRI

EFO_0008320.title: white matter volume measurement
EFO_0008320.description: quantification of the volume of white matter in the brain, usually through an MRI scan

EFO_0009805.title: whole body water mass
EFO_0009805.description: The weight of an individual's body water, i.e. the water content of a body that is contained in the tissues, the blood, the bones and elsewhere. The percentages of body water contained in various fluid compartments add up to total body water (TBW).

EFO_0009589.title: worry measurement
EFO_0009589.description: Quantification of whether an individual feels or felt worried.
